session(3) Library Functions Manual session(3)

NAME

session - Session

Detailed Description

This header provides a thin wrapper around the lib/audio/ system for session-level audio coordination. It handles the differences between host (mixing) and participant (simple capture/playback) modes.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Unified API for both host and participant audio handling
  • Host-only audio mixing for multi-participant sessions
  • Source management for tracking participant audio streams
  • Clean startup/shutdown coordination

USAGE: 

// Create audio context for a participant
session_audio_ctx_t *ctx = session_audio_create(false);
// Start capture and playback
session_audio_start_capture(ctx);
session_audio_start_playback(ctx);
// Cleanup
session_audio_destroy(ctx);

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

This header provides a unified interface for media capture that abstracts over webcam, file, stdin, and test pattern sources. It is designed to be reusable across different modes (client, mirror, and discovery mode).

CORE FEATURES:

  • Unified API for all media source types (webcam, file, stdin, test)
  • Configurable frame rate with adaptive sleep
  • Optional frame resizing for network transmission
  • FPS tracking and reporting
  • Loop support for file sources

USAGE: 

// Create capture context for webcam at 60 FPS
session_capture_config_t config = {


    .type = MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM,


    .path = "0",


    .target_fps = 60,


    .loop = false,


    .resize_for_network = false
};
session_capture_ctx_t *ctx = session_capture_create(&config);
// Read and process frames
while (!done) {


    image_t *frame = session_capture_read_frame(ctx);


    if (frame) {


        // Process frame...


    }


    session_capture_sleep_for_fps(ctx);
}
// Cleanup
session_capture_destroy(ctx);

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

This header provides a unified interface for terminal display that abstracts TTY detection, palette initialization, and frame rendering. It is designed to be reusable across different modes (client, mirror, and discovery mode).

CORE FEATURES:

  • TTY detection and management
  • Terminal capability detection (color, UTF-8)
  • Palette initialization and luminance mapping
  • Frame rendering with RLE expansion support
  • Snapshot mode support for single-frame capture

USAGE: 

// Create display context with color support
session_display_config_t config = {


    .snapshot_mode = false,


    .palette_type = PALETTE_STANDARD,


    .custom_palette = NULL,


    .color_mode = TERM_COLOR_AUTO
};
session_display_ctx_t *ctx = session_display_create(&config);
// Render frames
const char *frame_data = ...; // ASCII frame from server
session_display_render_frame(ctx, frame_data, false);
// Cleanup
session_display_destroy(ctx);

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

This header provides the server-side session hosting abstraction, encapsulating client management, connection acceptance, and event handling for session hosts.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Server lifecycle management (start, stop)
  • Client connection handling (add, remove, lookup)
  • Callback-based event notification
  • Encryption and authentication support

USAGE: 

// Define callbacks
void on_client_join(session_host_t *h, uint32_t id, void *data) {


    printf("Client %u joined\n", id);
}
void on_frame(session_host_t *h, uint32_t id, const image_t *frame, void *data) {


    // Process video frame from client
}
// Create and start host
session_host_config_t config = {


    .port = 27224,


    .max_clients = 32,


    .callbacks = { .on_client_join = on_client_join, .on_frame_received = on_frame }
};
session_host_t *h = session_host_create(&config);
session_host_start(h);
// Run until stopped...
// Cleanup
session_host_destroy(h);

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

Provides keyboard input handling for session-level controls including:

  • Media playback control (play/pause, seek)
  • Audio control (volume, mute)
  • Display control (color mode, webcam flip)
  • Help screen toggle

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

This header provides the client-side session participation abstraction, encapsulating connection management, media streaming, and event handling for session participants.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Connection lifecycle management (connect, disconnect, reconnect)
  • Video and audio stream control
  • Callback-based event notification
  • Encryption and authentication support

USAGE: 

// Define callbacks
void on_connected(session_participant_t *p, uint32_t id, void *data) {


    printf("Connected with ID %u\n", id);
}
void on_frame(session_participant_t *p, const char *frame, void *data) {


    // Render ASCII frame
}
// Create and connect
session_participant_config_t config = {


    .address = "127.0.0.1",


    .port = 27224,


    .callbacks = { .on_connected = on_connected, .on_frame_received = on_frame }
};
session_participant_t *p = session_participant_create(&config);
session_participant_connect(p);
// Start streaming
session_participant_start_video(p);
// Cleanup
session_participant_destroy(p);

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

This header provides settings structures and serialization functions for session configuration that can be transmitted between peers in discovery mode.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Compact binary serialization for network transmission
  • Version-based conflict detection
  • Bidirectional conversion with options system
  • Future-proof extensible structure with reserved bytes

USAGE: 

// Create settings from current options
session_settings_t settings;
session_settings_from_options(&settings);
// Serialize for transmission
uint8_t buffer[SESSION_SETTINGS_SERIALIZED_SIZE];
size_t len;
session_settings_serialize(&settings, buffer, &len);
// Deserialize received settings
session_settings_t received;
session_settings_deserialize(buffer, len, &received);
// Check if update needed
if (session_settings_needs_update(local_version, received.version)) {


    session_settings_apply_to_options(&received);
}

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

Provides a rolling-window FPS counter that measures the actual throughput of frame output operations (platform_write_all). Maintains a circular buffer of timestamps to compute stable FPS readings.

USAGE: 

fps_counter_t *fps = fps_counter_create();
// After each frame write to terminal
fps_counter_tick(fps);
// Get current FPS for display
float current_fps = fps_counter_get(fps);
fps_counter_destroy(fps);

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Provides an interactive keyboard help overlay that displays:

  • Available keyboard shortcuts
  • Current program state (volume, color mode, webcam flip, audio, mute status)

The keyboard help is toggled with '?' key and suppresses frame rendering while network reception continues in the background.

Display State:

  • Horizontally and vertically centered in terminal
  • Box-drawing border with UTF-8 fallback to ASCII
  • Real-time updates of option values (no network latency)

Threading:

  • Keyboard help state: Atomic bool for lock-free access
  • Terminal writes: Serialized via session_display_write_raw()
  • Option reads: Lock-free via RCU (GET_OPTION macro)

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

typedef struct session_server_like_config session_server_like_config_t

#include <src/common/session/server_like.h>Configuration for session_server_like_run()

All logging, terminal, and signal settings are read automatically from GET_OPTION() inside the function. Callers only provide the callbacks and user data below.

typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_server_like_init_fn) (void *user_data)

#include <src/common/session/server_like.h>Mode-specific initialization callback.

Called once after shared setup (keepawake, logging) completes. Should create sockets, load keys, start worker threads—everything the mode needs before its main accept loop starts.

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer from config.init_user_data

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK, or error code to abort (cleanup still runs)

Definition at line 68 of file server_like.h.

typedef void(* session_server_like_interrupt_fn) (void)

#include <src/common/session/server_like.h>Async-signal-safe interrupt callback.

Called from signal handler context (SIGINT / SIGTERM). Must only use async-signal-safe operations. Responsible for closing listening sockets to unblock the blocking run_fn (accept loop or poll).

Examples:

  • Server mode: close g_tcp_server listen sockets + websocket cancel
  • Discovery-Service mode: set g_server->tcp_server.running = false + acds_signal_exit()

Definition at line 98 of file server_like.h.

typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_server_like_run_fn) (void *user_data)

#include <src/common/session/server_like.h>Mode-specific blocking main loop.

Called after init_fn succeeds. Blocks until the server shuts down. Typical implementations:

  • Server mode: tcp_server_run(&g_tcp_server)
  • Discovery-Service mode: acds_server_run(&g_server)

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer from config.run_user_data

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK, or error code (cleanup always runs)

Definition at line 83 of file server_like.h.

typedef void(* session_server_like_status_fn) (void *user_data, ui_status_t *out_status)

#include <src/common/session/server_like.h>Status screen data callback.

Called at GET_OPTION(fps) Hz by the status screen thread. Should fill *out_status with current state for ui_status_display(). If NULL, the status screen is not shown even if --status-screen is set.

The callback receives a zeroed ui_status_t and must populate ALL fields it wants displayed (mode_name, addresses, port, connected_count, session_string, start_time, etc.). No static fields are inherited from the config — status_fn owns the entire ui_status_t.

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer from config.status_user_data
out_status Output struct to populate (pre-zeroed by caller, not NULL)

Definition at line 117 of file server_like.h.

#include <include/ascii-chat/ui/fps_counter.h>

Create a new FPS counter.

Returns

Pointer to counter, or NULL on failure

Creates and initializes a rolling-window FPS counter with a buffer for 30 frame timestamps.

Note

Call fps_counter_destroy() to free resources when done.

float
  fps_counter_get (fps_counter_t * counter)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/ui/fps_counter.h>


Get the current FPS value.


Parameters


counter FPS counter (can be NULL)


Returns


Current FPS as a float, or 0.0 if counter is NULL or
  insufficient data


Returns a rolling-window average FPS based on the last 30 frames.
    Stable FPS reading after at least 2 frames have been recorded.


Definition at line 74 of file fps_counter.c.


74                                               {
75   if (!counter || counter->count < 2) {
76     return 0.0f;
77   }
78 
79   // Find indices of oldest and newest timestamps in the buffer
80   int oldest_idx = (counter->head - counter->count + FPS_WINDOW_SIZE) % FPS_WINDOW_SIZE;
81   int newest_idx = (counter->head - 1 + FPS_WINDOW_SIZE) % FPS_WINDOW_SIZE;
82 
83   // Get the elapsed time between oldest and newest frames
84   uint64_t oldest_time = counter->frame_times[oldest_idx];
85   uint64_t newest_time = counter->frame_times[newest_idx];
86   uint64_t elapsed_ns = newest_time - oldest_time;
87 
88   // Avoid division by zero
89   if (elapsed_ns == 0) {
90     return 0.0f;
91   }
92 
93   // FPS = (number of frames) / elapsed_time
94   // We have (count - 1) frames across the elapsed time
95   // (count - 1) frames means count timestamps, so count-1 intervals
96   return (float)(counter->count - 1) * 1e9f / (float)elapsed_ns;
97 }






References fps_counter_s::count, FPS_WINDOW_SIZE,
    fps_counter_s::frame_times, and fps_counter_s::head.


Referenced by session_display_render_fps_overlay().






void
  fps_counter_tick (fps_counter_t * counter)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/ui/fps_counter.h>


Record a frame timestamp.


Parameters


counter FPS counter (must not be NULL)


Call this immediately after a frame write completes to record the
    output throughput. Uses nanosecond-precision timing.


Definition at line 57 of file fps_counter.c.


57                                               {
58   if (!counter) {
59     return;
60   }
61 
62   // Record current time at head position
63   counter->frame_times[counter->head] = time_get_ns();
64 
65   // Advance head pointer with wraparound
66   counter->head = (counter->head + 1) % FPS_WINDOW_SIZE;
67 
68   // Track how many valid entries we have (up to FPS_WINDOW_SIZE)
69   if (counter->count < FPS_WINDOW_SIZE) {
70     counter->count++;
71   }
72 }

void
  keyboard_help_render (session_display_ctx_t * ctx)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/ui/keyboard_help.h>


Render keyboard help TUI overlay.


Parameters


ctx Display context (must not be NULL)


Renders a centered keyboard help screen showing:


    
  
  • Keyboard shortcuts and their functions
    • 
  
  • Current program state values:
    • 




  
 •

  
Volume (displayed as bar graph:
      ████████░░
      80%)

  
 •

  
Color mode (Mono, 16-color, 256-color, Truecolor)

  
 •

  
Webcam flip state (Normal or Flipped)

  
 •

  
Audio status (Enabled or Disabled)

  
 •

  
Mute status (in volume display)




Layout:


    
  
  • Centered horizontally: col = (term_width - box_width) / 2
    • 
  
  • Centered vertically: row = (term_height - box_height) / 2
    • 
  
  • Border: Box drawing chars
      (╔═╗║╚╝) with ASCII fallback
      (+|-) for UTF-8-less terminals
    • 



Edge Cases:


    
  
  • Terminal too small: Shows simplified help or warning
    • 
  
  • Non-TTY: Skips rendering (no terminal for display)
    • 



Note


Reads live option values via GET_OPTION() - values
  reflect current state



Uses session_display_write_raw() for atomic terminal
  output




Render keyboard help TUI overlay.


Definition at line 318 of file keyboard_help.c.


318                                                       {
319   if (!ctx) {
320     log_error("keyboard_help_render: ctx is NULL!");
321     return;
322   }
323 
324   log_info("keyboard_help_render: STARTING");
325 
326   // Get terminal dimensions
327   int term_width = (int)terminal_get_effective_width();
328   int term_height = (int)terminal_get_effective_height();
329   log_info("keyboard_help_render: term_width=%d, term_height=%d", term_width, term_height);
330 
331   // Use available terminal width, capped at preferred width
332   int box_width = term_width;
333   if (box_width > 48)
334     box_width = 48; // Cap at preferred width
335   if (box_width < 30)
336     box_width = 30; // Absolute minimum for readability
337 
338   // Calculate centering position (true mathematical centering)
339   // Horizontal centering
340   int start_col = (term_width - box_width) / 2;
341   if (start_col < 0) {
342     start_col = 0;
343   }
344 
345   // Calculate box height dynamically based on content
346   // With animations separator line added, the standard help screen is 25 rows
347   int box_height = 25;
348 
349   // Vertical centering with dynamic box height
350   int start_row = (term_height - box_height) / 2 - 3; // -3 offset for proper vertical centering
351   if (start_row < 0) {
352     start_row = 0;
353   }
354 
355   // Build help screen content
356   const size_t BUFFER_SIZE = 8192; // Increased from 4096 to ensure all content fits
357   char *buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(BUFFER_SIZE, char *);
358   size_t buf_pos = 0;
359 
360 #define APPEND(fmt, ...)                                                                                               \
361   do {                                                                                                                 \
362     int written = snprintf(buffer + buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE - buf_pos, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                               \
363     if (written > 0) {                                                                                                 \
364       buf_pos += written;                                                                                              \
365     }                                                                                                                  \
366   } while (0)
367 
368   // Clear screen and position cursor
369   APPEND("\033[2J"); // Clear screen
370   APPEND("\033[H");  // Cursor to home
371 
372   // Build help screen with proper spacing using UTF-8 width-aware padding
373   char line_buf[256];
374   char border_buf[256];
375 
376   // Generate top border
377   APPEND("\033[%d;%dH", start_row + 1, start_col + 1);
378   border_buf[0] = '\0';
379   int border_pos = 0;
380   int result = SAFE_SNPRINTF(border_buf + border_pos, sizeof(border_buf) - border_pos, "%s", "╔");
381   if (result < 0) {
382     log_error("Failed to write top-left corner: snprintf returned %d", result);
383   } else {
384     border_pos += result;
385   }
386   for (int i = 1; i < box_width - 1; i++) {
387     result = SAFE_SNPRINTF(border_buf + border_pos, sizeof(border_buf) - border_pos, "%s", "═");
388     if (result < 0) {
389       log_error("Failed to write horizontal line: snprintf returned %d", result);
390       break;
391     }
392     border_pos += result;
393   }
394   result = SAFE_SNPRINTF(border_buf + border_pos, sizeof(border_buf) - border_pos, "%s", "╗");
395   if (result < 0) {
396     log_error("Failed to write top-right corner: snprintf returned %d", result);
397   }
398   APPEND("%s", border_buf);
399 
400   // Title
401   APPEND("\033[%d;%dH", start_row + 2, start_col + 1);
402   build_help_line(line_buf, sizeof(line_buf), "ascii-chat Keyboard Shortcuts", box_width);
403   APPEND("%s", line_buf);
404 
405   // Generate separator border
406   APPEND("\033[%d;%dH", start_row + 3, start_col + 1);
407   border_buf[0] = '\0';
408   border_pos = 0;
409   result = SAFE_SNPRINTF(border_buf + border_pos, sizeof(border_buf) - border_pos, "%s", "╠");
410   if (result < 0) {
411     log_error("Failed to write left T: snprintf returned %d", result);
412   } else {
413     border_pos += result;
414   }
415   for (int i = 1; i < box_width - 1; i++) {
416     result = SAFE_SNPRINTF(border_buf + border_pos, sizeof(border_buf) - border_pos, "%s", "═");
417     if (result < 0) {
418       log_error("Failed to write horizontal line: snprintf returned %d", result);
419       break;
420     }
421     border_pos += result;
422   }
423   result = SAFE_SNPRINTF(border_buf + border_pos, sizeof(border_buf) - border_pos, "%s", "╣");
424   if (result < 0) {
425     log_error("Failed to write right T: snprintf returned %d", result);
426   }
427   APPEND("%s", border_buf);
428 
429   // Navigation section
430   int current_row = 4;
431   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width,
432                    "Navigation & Control:");
433   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width,
434                    "─────────────────────");
435   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width,
436                    "?       Toggle this help screen");
437   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width,
438                    "Esc     Close help / Quit app");
439 
440   // Check if media is provided (only show Space/Seek keys if media is loaded)
441   const char *media_url = GET_OPTION(media_url);
442   const char *media_file = GET_OPTION(media_file);
443   bool has_media = (media_url && strlen(media_url) > 0) || (media_file && strlen(media_file) > 0);
444 
445   if (has_media) {
446     append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width,
447                      "Space   Play/Pause (files only)");
448     append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width,
449                      "← / →   Seek backward/forward 30s");
450   }
451 
452   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width,
453                    "m / M   Mute/Unmute audio");
454   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width,
455                    "↑ / ↓   Volume up/down (10%)");
456   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width,
457                    "c / C   Cycle color mode");
458   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width,
459                    "f / F   Cycle color filter");
460   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width,
461                    "x / X   Flip webcam horizontally");
462   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width,
463                    "y / Y   Flip webcam vertically");
464   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width,
465                    "r / R   Cycle render mode");
466 
467 #ifndef NDEBUG
468   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width,
469                    "`       Print current sync primitive state");
470 #endif
471 
472   // Blank line before settings section
473   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width, "");
474 
475   // Current settings section
476   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width, "Current Settings:");
477   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width, "───────────────");
478 
479   // Get current option values
480   double current_volume = GET_OPTION(speakers_volume);
481   int current_color_mode = (int)GET_OPTION(color_mode);
482   int current_render_mode = (int)GET_OPTION(render_mode);
483   color_filter_t current_color_filter = GET_OPTION(color_filter);
484   bool flip_x = (bool)GET_OPTION(flip_x);
485   bool flip_y = (bool)GET_OPTION(flip_y);
486   bool current_audio = (bool)GET_OPTION(audio_enabled);
487 
488   // Format volume bar as "[========  ] 80%"
489   char volume_bar[32];
490   format_volume_bar(current_volume, volume_bar, sizeof(volume_bar));
491 
492   // Get string values
493   const char *color_str = color_mode_to_string(current_color_mode);
494   const char *filter_str = color_filter_to_string(current_color_filter);
495   const char *render_str = render_mode_to_string(current_render_mode);
496 
497   // Create status indicators for flip, audio, and matrix rain
498   // Format flip status as "rows=X/O cols=X/O" (rows=flip_y, cols=flip_x)
499   char flip_status[64];
500   snprintf(flip_status, sizeof(flip_status), "rows=%s cols=%s", status_indicator(flip_y), status_indicator(flip_x));
501 
502   const char *audio_text = status_indicator(current_audio);
503   bool matrix_rain_enabled = GET_OPTION(matrix_rain);
504   const char *matrix_text = status_indicator(matrix_rain_enabled);
505 
506   // Build settings lines with UTF-8 width-aware padding (ordered to match keybinds: m, ↑/↓, c, f, x/y, r)
507   append_settings_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width, "Audio",
508                        audio_text, 6);
509   append_settings_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width, "Volume",
510                        volume_bar, 6);
511   append_settings_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width, "Color", color_str,
512                        6);
513   append_settings_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width, "Filter",
514                        filter_str, 6);
515   append_settings_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width, "Render",
516                        render_str, 6);
517   append_settings_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width, "Flip",
518                        flip_status, 6);
519 
520   // Blank line before animations section
521   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width, "");
522 
523   // Animations section
524   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width,
525                    "Animations (number key toggle):");
526   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width,
527                    "───────────────────────────────");
528 
529   // Format: "(0) Matrix \"Digital Rain\" : X/O"
530   char animation_line[256];
531   snprintf(animation_line, sizeof(animation_line), "(0) Matrix \"Digital Rain\" : %s", matrix_text);
532   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width, animation_line);
533 
534   // FPS Counter toggle
535   char fps_line[256];
536   snprintf(fps_line, sizeof(fps_line), "(-) FPS Counter : %s", status_indicator(GET_OPTION(fps_counter)));
537   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width, fps_line);
538 
539   // Blank line before footer
540   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width, "");
541 
542   // Footer
543   append_help_line(buffer, &buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE, start_row, &current_row, start_col, box_width, "Press ? to close");
544 
545   // Bottom border
546   int remaining_buf = BUFFER_SIZE - buf_pos;
547   int written = snprintf(buffer + buf_pos, remaining_buf, "\033[%d;%dH", start_row + current_row, start_col + 1);
548   if (written > 0) {
549     buf_pos += written;
550   }
551   border_buf[0] = '\0';
552   border_pos = 0;
553   result = SAFE_SNPRINTF(border_buf + border_pos, sizeof(border_buf) - border_pos, "%s", "╚");
554   if (result < 0) {
555     log_error("Failed to write bottom-left corner: snprintf returned %d", result);
556   } else {
557     border_pos += result;
558   }
559   for (int i = 1; i < box_width - 1; i++) {
560     result = SAFE_SNPRINTF(border_buf + border_pos, sizeof(border_buf) - border_pos, "%s", "═");
561     if (result < 0) {
562       log_error("Failed to write horizontal line: snprintf returned %d", result);
563       break;
564     }
565     border_pos += result;
566   }
567   result = SAFE_SNPRINTF(border_buf + border_pos, sizeof(border_buf) - border_pos, "%s", "╝");
568   if (result < 0) {
569     log_error("Failed to write bottom-right corner: snprintf returned %d", result);
570   }
571   written = snprintf(buffer + buf_pos, BUFFER_SIZE - buf_pos, "%s", border_buf);
572   if (written > 0) {
573     buf_pos += written;
574   }
575 
576 #undef APPEND
577 
578   log_info("keyboard_help_render: buffer prepared, buf_pos=%zu", buf_pos);
579 
580   // Write buffer to terminal
581   session_display_write_raw(ctx, buffer, buf_pos);
582   log_info("keyboard_help_render: buffer written to terminal");
583 
584   // Flush output
585   if (ctx && session_display_has_tty(ctx)) {
586     int tty_fd = session_display_get_tty_fd(ctx);
587     log_info("keyboard_help_render: tty_fd=%d", tty_fd);
588     if (tty_fd >= 0) {
589       (void)terminal_flush(tty_fd);
590       log_info("keyboard_help_render: terminal flushed");
591     }
592   }
593 
594   SAFE_FREE(buffer);
595   log_info("keyboard_help_render: COMPLETE");
596 }

Add an audio source for mixing (host only).


Parameters


ctx Audio context (must not be NULL, must be host
  context)


source_id Unique identifier for this source


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Registers a new audio source for the mixer. Each participant

Note


Only available when context was created with
  is_host=true.


Definition at line 225 of file
    src/common/session/audio.c.


225                                                                                          {
226   if (!ctx || !ctx->initialized) {
227     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_audio_add_source: invalid context");
228   }
229 
230   if (!ctx->is_host) {
231     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "session_audio_add_source: not a host context");
232   }
233 
234   // Check if source already exists
235   for (int i = 0; i < SESSION_AUDIO_MAX_SOURCES; i++) {
236     if (ctx->sources[i].active && ctx->sources[i].source_id == source_id) {
237       return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Already registered
238     }
239   }
240 
241   // Find empty slot
242   for (int i = 0; i < SESSION_AUDIO_MAX_SOURCES; i++) {
243     if (!ctx->sources[i].active) {
244       ctx->sources[i].source_id = source_id;
245       ctx->sources[i].active = true;
246       ctx->sources[i].buffer = audio_ring_buffer_create();
247       if (!ctx->sources[i].buffer) {
248         ctx->sources[i].active = false;
249         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create audio buffer for source");
250       }
251       ctx->source_count++;
252       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
253     }
254   }
255 
256   return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, "Maximum audio sources reached");
257 }

Creates and initializes a session audio context. Hosts get audio

#include <src/common/session/audio.h>

ctx Audio context to destroy (can be NULL)

117 
118   // Cleanup host-specific resources

120     // Destroy source buffers
121     for (int i = 0; i < SESSION_AUDIO_MAX_SOURCES; i++) {
122       if (ctx->sources[i].buffer) {

124         ctx->sources[i].buffer = NULL;
125       }

127 
128     // Free mix buffer
129     if (ctx->mix_buffer) {
130       SAFE_FREE(ctx->mix_buffer);
131     }
132   }
133 
134   // Destroy underlying audio context
135   audio_destroy(&ctx->audio_ctx);
136 
137   ctx->initialized = false;
138   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
139 }

size_t
  session_audio_mix_excluding (session_audio_ctx_t * ctx, uint32_t
  exclude_id, float * output, size_t num_samples)


#include <src/common/session/audio.h>


Mix all sources except one and return the result (host only).


Parameters


ctx Audio context (must not be NULL, must be host
  context)


exclude_id Source ID to exclude from mix (for echo prevention)


output Output buffer for mixed audio (must not be NULL)


num_samples Number of samples to mix


Returns


Number of samples actually mixed


Mixes audio from all registered sources except exclude_id. This
    allows sending each participant a mix of all other participants without
    their own audio (preventing echo).




Note


Only available when context was created with
  is_host=true.


Definition at line 301 of file
    src/common/session/audio.c.


301                                                                                                                      {
302   if (!ctx || !ctx->initialized || !output || num_samples == 0) {
303     return 0;
304   }
305 
306   if (!ctx->is_host) {
307     return 0;
308   }
309 
310   // Initialize output to silence
311   memset(output, 0, num_samples * sizeof(float));
312 
313   // Ensure we have a mix buffer
314   if (!ctx->mix_buffer || num_samples > ctx->mix_buffer_size) {
315     return 0;
316   }
317 
318   int sources_mixed = 0;
319 
320   // Mix all active sources except the excluded one
321   for (int i = 0; i < SESSION_AUDIO_MAX_SOURCES; i++) {
322     if (!ctx->sources[i].active || ctx->sources[i].source_id == exclude_id) {
323       continue;
324     }
325 
326     if (!ctx->sources[i].buffer) {
327       continue;
328     }
329 
330     // Read samples from this source
331     size_t samples_read = audio_ring_buffer_read(ctx->sources[i].buffer, ctx->mix_buffer, num_samples);
332 
333     if (samples_read == 0) {
334       continue;
335     }
336 
337     // Add to output mix
338     for (size_t j = 0; j < samples_read; j++) {
339       output[j] += ctx->mix_buffer[j];
340     }
341 
342     sources_mixed++;
343   }
344 
345   // Apply simple clipping prevention if multiple sources were mixed
346   if (sources_mixed > 1) {
347     float scale = 1.0f / (float)sources_mixed;
348     for (size_t i = 0; i < num_samples; i++) {
349       output[i] *= scale;
350       // Soft clipping
351       if (output[i] > 1.0f) {
352         output[i] = 1.0f;
353       } else if (output[i] < -1.0f) {
354         output[i] = -1.0f;
355       }
356     }
357   }
358 
359   return num_samples;
360 }

    session_audio_ctx::initialized, session_audio_ctx::is_host,
    session_audio_ctx::mix_buffer,
    session_audio_ctx::mix_buffer_size, SESSION_AUDIO_MAX_SOURCES,
    session_audio_source_t::source_id, and
    session_audio_ctx::sources.

#include <src/common/session/audio.h>

282   }
283 
284   if (!ctx->is_host) {
285     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "session_audio_write_source: not a host context");
286   }
287 
288   // Find the source
289   for (int i = 0; i < SESSION_AUDIO_MAX_SOURCES; i++) {
290     if (ctx->sources[i].active && ctx->sources[i].source_id == source_id) {
291       if (ctx->sources[i].buffer) {
292         return audio_ring_buffer_write(ctx->sources[i].buffer, samples, (int)num_samples);
293       }
294       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Source buffer not initialized");
295     }
296   }
297 
298   return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "Audio source not found: %u", source_id);
299 }

  session_capture_config_t * config)


#include <src/common/session/capture.h>


Legacy function - creates either mirror or network capture based

193                                                                                       {
194   // Auto-create config from command-line options if NULL
195   session_capture_config_t auto_config = {0};
196   if (!config) {
197     // Auto-initialization from GET_OPTION() only works when options have been parsed
198     // Verify by checking if we can access options (would SET_ERRNO if not)
199     const char *media_file = GET_OPTION(media_file);
200     bool media_from_stdin = GET_OPTION(media_from_stdin);
201 
202     if (media_file[0] != '\0') {
203       // File or stdin streaming
204       auto_config.type = media_from_stdin ? MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN : MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE;
205       auto_config.path = media_file;
206       auto_config.loop = GET_OPTION(media_loop) && !media_from_stdin;
207     } else if (GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
208       // Test pattern mode
209       auto_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST;
210       auto_config.path = NULL;
211     } else {
212       // Webcam mode (default)
213       static char webcam_index_str[32];
214       unsigned int idx = GET_OPTION(webcam_index);
215       safe_snprintf(webcam_index_str, sizeof(webcam_index_str), "%u", idx);
216       auto_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM;
217       auto_config.path = webcam_index_str;
218       log_debug("Webcam mode: using device index %u", idx);
219     }
220 
221     // Default settings suitable for local display
222     auto_config.target_fps = 60;
223     auto_config.resize_for_network = false;
224     config = &auto_config;
225   }
226 
227   // Check if we should exit before starting initialization
228   if (config->should_exit_callback && config->should_exit_callback(config->callback_data)) {
229     return NULL;
230   }
231 
232   // Allocate context
233   session_capture_ctx_t *ctx = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(session_capture_ctx_t), session_capture_ctx_t *);
234 
235   // Store configuration
236   ctx->target_fps = config->target_fps > 0 ? config->target_fps : 60;
237   ctx->resize_for_network = config->resize_for_network;
238 
239   // Store audio configuration
240   ctx->audio_enabled = config->enable_audio;
241   ctx->audio_fallback_enabled = config->audio_fallback_to_mic;
242   ctx->mic_audio_ctx = config->mic_audio_ctx;
243   ctx->using_file_audio = false;
244   ctx->file_has_audio = false;
245 
246   // Create media source from config type and path
247   ctx->source = media_source_create(config->type, config->path);
248 
249   if (!ctx->source) {
250     // Preserve existing error if set, otherwise set generic error
251     asciichat_error_t existing_error = GET_ERRNO();
252     if (existing_error == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
253       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Failed to create media source");
254     }
255     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
256     return NULL;
257   }
258 
259   // Set up exit callback on media source for graceful shutdown during I/O
260   // This allows Ctrl+C to abort blocking FFmpeg calls like YouTube HTTP requests
261   if (config->should_exit_callback && ctx->source) {
262     media_source_set_exit_callback(ctx->source, config->should_exit_callback, config->callback_data);
263   }
264 
265   // Detect if media source has audio
266   if (ctx->audio_enabled && ctx->source) {
267     ctx->file_has_audio = media_source_has_audio(ctx->source);
268     if (ctx->file_has_audio) {
269       ctx->using_file_audio = true;
270       log_info("Audio capture enabled: using file audio");
271     } else if (ctx->audio_fallback_enabled && ctx->mic_audio_ctx) {
272       ctx->using_file_audio = false;
273       log_info("Audio capture enabled: file has no audio, using microphone fallback");
274     } else {
275       ctx->audio_enabled = false;
276       log_debug("Audio capture disabled: no file audio and no fallback configured");
277     }
278   }
279 
280   // Enable loop if requested (only for file sources)
281   if (config->loop && config->type == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE) {
282     media_source_set_loop(ctx->source, true);
283   }
284 
285   // Set initial pause state if requested (only for file sources)
286   // Note: We don't pause yet - we'll pause AFTER reading the first frame in the render loop
287   // This is because paused sources return NULL from read_video()
288   if (config->type == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE && GET_OPTION(pause)) {
289     ctx->should_pause_after_first_frame = true;
290     log_debug("Will pause after first frame (--pause flag)");
291   }
292 
293   // Perform initial seek if requested
294   if (config->initial_seek_timestamp > 0.0) {
295     log_debug("Seeking to %.2f seconds", config->initial_seek_timestamp);
296     asciichat_error_t seek_err = media_source_seek(ctx->source, config->initial_seek_timestamp);
297     if (seek_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
298       log_warn("Failed to seek to %.2f seconds: %d", config->initial_seek_timestamp, seek_err);
299       // Continue anyway - seeking is best-effort
300     } else {
301       log_debug("Successfully seeked to %.2f seconds", config->initial_seek_timestamp);
302       // Codec buffers are flushed during seek, so next frame read will use correct position
303 
304       // Reset timing state after seek to prevent FPS calculation confusion
305       // (frame_count and elapsed_time must match the new playback position)
306       ctx->frame_count = 0;
307       ctx->start_time_ns = time_get_ns();
308 
309     }
310   }
311 
312   // Initialize adaptive sleep for frame rate limiting
313   // Calculate baseline sleep time in nanoseconds from target FPS
314   uint64_t baseline_sleep_ns = NS_PER_SEC_INT / ctx->target_fps;
315   adaptive_sleep_config_t sleep_config = {
316       .baseline_sleep_ns = baseline_sleep_ns,
317       .min_speed_multiplier = 0.5, // Allow slowing down to 50% of baseline
318       .max_speed_multiplier = 2.0, // Allow speeding up to 200% of baseline
319       .speedup_rate = 0.1,         // Adapt by 10% per frame if possible
320       .slowdown_rate = 0.1         // Adapt by 10% per frame if possible
321   };
322   adaptive_sleep_init(&ctx->sleep_state, &sleep_config);
323 
324   // Initialize FPS tracker
325   // Note: Must allocate tracker_name on heap since fps_init stores a pointer to it
326   char *tracker_name = SAFE_MALLOC(32, char *);
327   if (!tracker_name) {
328     media_source_destroy(ctx->source);
329     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
330     return NULL;
331   }
332   safe_snprintf(tracker_name, 32, "CAPTURE_%u", ctx->target_fps);
333   fps_init(&ctx->fps_tracker, (int)ctx->target_fps, tracker_name);
334 
335   // Record start time for FPS calculation (nanoseconds)
336   ctx->start_time_ns = time_get_ns();
337 
338   ctx->initialized = true;
339   return ctx;
340 }

  (session_capture_ctx_t * ctx, image_t * frame)


#include <src/common/session/capture.h>


Process a frame for network transmission (resize if needed).


Parameters


ctx Capture context (must not be NULL)


frame Input frame from
  session_capture_read_frame() (must not be NULL)


Returns


Processed image ready for transmission, or NULL on
  error


Resizes the frame to network-optimal dimensions if
    resize_for_network was enabled in the configuration. The returned image is a
    new allocation owned by the caller and must be freed with
    image_destroy().




Note


Returns a copy if no resizing is needed.



Caller owns the returned image and must call
    image_destroy().

409                                                                                               {
410   if (!ctx || !frame) {
411     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_capture_process_for_transmission: NULL parameter");
412     return NULL;
413   }
414 
415   // If resize is not enabled, just create a copy
416   if (!ctx->resize_for_network) {
417     image_t *copy = image_new(frame->w, frame->h);
418     if (!copy) {
419       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate image copy");
420       return NULL;
421     }
422     memcpy(copy->pixels, frame->pixels, (size_t)frame->w * (size_t)frame->h * sizeof(rgb_pixel_t));
423     return copy;
424   }
425 
426   // Calculate optimal dimensions for network transmission
427   ssize_t resized_width, resized_height;
428   calculate_optimal_dimensions(frame->w, frame->h, SESSION_MAX_FRAME_WIDTH, SESSION_MAX_FRAME_HEIGHT, &resized_width,
429                                &resized_height);
430 
431   // Check if resizing is actually needed
432   if (frame->w == resized_width && frame->h == resized_height) {
433     // No resizing needed - create a copy
434     image_t *copy = image_new(frame->w, frame->h);
435     if (!copy) {
436       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate image copy");
437       return NULL;
438     }
439     memcpy(copy->pixels, frame->pixels, (size_t)frame->w * (size_t)frame->h * sizeof(rgb_pixel_t));
440     return copy;
441   }
442 
443   // Create new image for resized frame
444   image_t *resized = image_new(resized_width, resized_height);
445   if (!resized) {
446     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate resized image buffer");
447     return NULL;
448   }
449 
450   // Perform resizing operation
451   image_resize(frame, resized);
452 
453   return resized;
454 }

image_t * session_capture_read_frame
  (session_capture_ctx_t * ctx)


#include <src/common/session/capture.h>


Read the next video frame from the capture source.


Parameters


ctx Capture context (must not be NULL)


Returns


Pointer to image frame, or NULL on error/end


Reads the next video frame from the media source. The returned
    image is owned by the media source and should NOT be freed by the
  caller.




Note


Frame is valid until next call or context destruction.



Check session_capture_at_end() to distinguish error from
    EOF.

367                                                                 {

369     return NULL;
370   }
371 
372   static uint64_t last_frame_time_ns = 0;
373   uint64_t frame_request_time_ns = time_get_ns();
374 
375   image_t *frame = media_source_read_video(ctx->source);
376 
377   if (frame) {
378     uint64_t frame_available_time_ns = time_get_ns();
379 
380     // Track frame for FPS reporting
381     fps_frame_ns(&ctx->fps_tracker, frame_available_time_ns, "frame captured");
382     ctx->frame_count++;
383 
384     // Log frame-to-frame timing
385     if (last_frame_time_ns > 0) {
386       uint64_t time_since_last_frame_ns = time_elapsed_ns(last_frame_time_ns, frame_request_time_ns);
387       uint64_t time_to_get_frame_ns = time_elapsed_ns(frame_request_time_ns, frame_available_time_ns);
388       double since_last_ms = (double)time_since_last_frame_ns / NS_PER_MS;
389       double to_get_ms = (double)time_to_get_frame_ns / NS_PER_MS;
390 
391       if (ctx->frame_count % 30 == 0) {
392         log_dev_every(3 * US_PER_SEC_INT, "FRAME_TIMING[%lu]: since_last=%.1f ms, to_get=%.1f ms", ctx->frame_count,
393                       since_last_ms, to_get_ms);
394       }
395     }
396     last_frame_time_ns = frame_available_time_ns;
397 
398     // Handle --pause flag: pause after first frame is read
399     if (ctx->should_pause_after_first_frame && !ctx->paused_after_first_frame) {
400       media_source_pause(ctx->source);
401       ctx->paused_after_first_frame = true;
402       log_info("Paused (--pause flag)");
403     }
404   }
405 
406   return frame;
407 }

459   }
460 
461   // For file/stdin sources: use direct FPS sleep (no adaptation)
462   // This avoids the adaptive sleep multipliers which cause 2x slowdown
463   media_source_type_t source_type = media_source_get_type(ctx->source);
464   if (source_type == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE || source_type == MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN) {
465     uint32_t target_fps = session_capture_get_target_fps(ctx);
466     if (target_fps > 0) {
467       uint64_t sleep_ns = NS_PER_SEC_INT / target_fps;
468       platform_sleep_ns(sleep_ns);
469     }
470     return;
471   }
472 
473   // For network/webcam sources: use adaptive sleep to handle variable network conditions
474   // queue_depth=0, target_depth=0 maintains constant frame rate
475   adaptive_sleep_do(&ctx->sleep_state, 0, 0);
476 }






References adaptive_sleep_do(),
    session_capture_ctx::initialized, MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE,
    media_source_get_type(), MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN,
    NS_PER_SEC_INT, platform_sleep_ns(),
    session_capture_get_target_fps(),
    session_capture_ctx::sleep_state, and
    session_capture_ctx::source.

void
  session_display_clear (session_display_ctx_t * ctx)


#include <src/common/session/display.h>


Clear the terminal screen.


Parameters


ctx Display context (must not be NULL)


Clears the terminal screen and moves cursor to home position.

1057                                                        {
1058   if (!ctx || !ctx->initialized) {
1059     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session display context is NULL or uninitialized");
1060     return;
1061   }
1062 
1063   // Skip terminal clear in snapshot mode to preserve rendered output
1064   if (ctx->snapshot_mode) {
1065     return;
1066   }
1067 
1068   // Only perform terminal operations when we have a valid TTY (not when piping)
1069   if (ctx->has_tty && ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) {
1070     (void)terminal_clear_screen();
1071     (void)terminal_cursor_home(ctx->tty_info.fd);
1072   }

char
  * session_display_convert_to_ascii (session_display_ctx_t * ctx, const
  image_t * image)


#include <src/common/session/display.h>


Convert an image to ASCII art using display context and
    command-line options.


Parameters


ctx Display context (must not be NULL)


image Image to convert (must not be NULL)


Returns


Dynamically allocated ASCII string, or NULL on
  error


Converts the given image to ASCII art using:


    
  
  • Palette and terminal capabilities from display context
    • 
  
  • Width, height, stretch, and aspect ratio settings from
    GET_OPTION()
    • 



This completely encapsulates ASCII conversion complexity so
    callers don't need to manage palette, terminal capabilities, or conversion
    options.




The returned string must be freed by caller with
    SAFE_FREE().


Definition at line 509 of file
    src/common/session/display.c.


509                                                                                          {
510   if (!ctx) {
511     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_display_convert_to_ascii: ctx is NULL");
512     return NULL;
513   }
514 
515   if (!ctx->initialized) {
516     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "session_display_convert_to_ascii: ctx not initialized");
517     return NULL;
518   }
519 
520   if (!image) {
521     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_display_convert_to_ascii: image is NULL");
522     return NULL;
523   }
524 
525   // Get conversion parameters from command-line options
526   unsigned short int width = GET_OPTION(width);
527   unsigned short int height = GET_OPTION(height);
528   bool stretch = GET_OPTION(stretch);
529   bool preserve_aspect_ratio = !stretch;
530 
531   // Determine if we should apply flip_x and flip_y
532   bool flip_x_enabled = GET_OPTION(flip_x);
533   bool flip_y_enabled = GET_OPTION(flip_y);
534 
535   color_filter_t color_filter = GET_OPTION(color_filter);
536 
537   // Handle dynamic matrix rain effect toggle
538   bool matrix_rain_enabled = GET_OPTION(matrix_rain);
539   if (matrix_rain_enabled && !ctx->digital_rain) {
540     // Initialize digital rain if it's now enabled but wasn't before
541     unsigned short int width_us = terminal_get_effective_width();
542     unsigned short int height_us = terminal_get_effective_height();
543     int width_for_rain = (int)width_us;
544     int height_for_rain = (int)height_us;
545     ctx->digital_rain = digital_rain_init(width_for_rain, height_for_rain);
546     if (ctx->digital_rain) {
547       digital_rain_set_color_from_filter(ctx->digital_rain, color_filter);
548       log_info("Matrix rain effect: enabled");
549     }
550   } else if (!matrix_rain_enabled && ctx->digital_rain) {
551     // Disable digital rain if it's now disabled but was enabled before
552     digital_rain_destroy(ctx->digital_rain);
553     ctx->digital_rain = NULL;
554     log_info("Matrix rain effect: disabled");
555   }
556 
557   // Make a mutable copy of terminal capabilities for ascii_convert_with_capabilities
558   terminal_capabilities_t caps_copy = ctx->caps;
559 
560   // Re-evaluate render_mode and color_level on every frame to pick up live changes
561   caps_copy.render_mode = (render_mode_t)GET_OPTION(render_mode);
562   terminal_color_mode_t color_mode_opt = (terminal_color_mode_t)GET_OPTION(color_mode);
563   if (color_mode_opt != TERM_COLOR_AUTO) {
564     caps_copy.color_level = color_mode_opt;
565   }
566 
567   // MEASURE EVERY OPERATION - Debug systematic timing
568   uint64_t t_flip_start = time_get_ns();
569 
570   // Apply horizontal and/or vertical flips if requested
571   image_t *flipped_image = NULL;
572   const image_t *display_image = image;
573 
574   if ((flip_x_enabled || flip_y_enabled) && image->w > 1 && image->h > 1 && image->pixels) {
575     START_TIMER("image_flip");
576     uint64_t t_flip_alloc_start = time_get_ns();
577     flipped_image = image_new((size_t)image->w, (size_t)image->h);
578     uint64_t t_flip_alloc_end = time_get_ns();
579 
580     if (flipped_image) {
581       uint64_t t_flip_memcpy_start = time_get_ns();
582       // OPTIMIZATION: Copy entire image first (sequential memory access - cache-friendly)
583       memcpy(flipped_image->pixels, image->pixels, (size_t)image->w * (size_t)image->h * sizeof(rgb_pixel_t));
584       uint64_t t_flip_memcpy_end = time_get_ns();
585 
586       uint64_t t_flip_reverse_start = time_get_ns();
587 
588       // Apply horizontal flip (X-axis)
589       if (flip_x_enabled) {
590 #if SIMD_SUPPORT_NEON
591         // Use NEON-accelerated flip on ARM processors
592         image_flip_horizontal_neon(flipped_image);
593 #else
594         // Scalar fallback for non-NEON systems
595         for (int y = 0; y < image->h; y++) {
596           rgb_pixel_t *row = &flipped_image->pixels[y * image->w];
597           for (int x = 0; x < image->w / 2; x++) {
598             rgb_pixel_t temp = row[x];
599             row[x] = row[image->w - 1 - x];
600             row[image->w - 1 - x] = temp;
601           }
602         }
603 #endif
604       }
605 
606       // Apply vertical flip (Y-axis)
607       if (flip_y_enabled) {
608         for (int y = 0; y < image->h / 2; y++) {
609           rgb_pixel_t *top_row = &flipped_image->pixels[y * image->w];
610           rgb_pixel_t *bottom_row = &flipped_image->pixels[(image->h - 1 - y) * image->w];
611           for (int x = 0; x < image->w; x++) {
612             rgb_pixel_t temp = top_row[x];
613             top_row[x] = bottom_row[x];
614             bottom_row[x] = temp;
615           }
616         }
617       }
618 
619       uint64_t t_flip_reverse_end = time_get_ns();
620       display_image = flipped_image;
621 
622       log_dev("TIMING_FLIP: alloc=%llu us, memcpy=%llu us, flip=%llu us (x=%d, y=%d)",
623               (t_flip_alloc_end - t_flip_alloc_start) / 1000, (t_flip_memcpy_end - t_flip_memcpy_start) / 1000,
624               (t_flip_reverse_end - t_flip_reverse_start) / 1000, flip_x_enabled, flip_y_enabled);
625     }
626     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, 3 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, 3 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "image_flip",
627                              "IMAGE_FLIP: Flip complete (%.2f ms)");
628   }
629   uint64_t t_flip_end = time_get_ns();
630 
631   uint64_t t_filter_start = time_get_ns();
632 
633   // Apply color filter to RGB pixels before ASCII conversion
634   // Rainbow filter is handled separately via ANSI color replacement
635   image_t *filtered_image = NULL;
636   if (color_filter != COLOR_FILTER_NONE && color_filter != COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW) {
637     // Create a copy to avoid modifying the original
638     filtered_image = image_new((size_t)display_image->w, (size_t)display_image->h);
639     if (filtered_image && filtered_image->pixels && display_image->pixels) {
640       memcpy(filtered_image->pixels, display_image->pixels,
641              (size_t)display_image->w * (size_t)display_image->h * sizeof(rgb_pixel_t));
642 
643       // Apply filter to the copy
644       int stride = (int)display_image->w * 3;
645       float time_seconds = (float)t_filter_start / (float)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
646       apply_color_filter((uint8_t *)filtered_image->pixels, (uint32_t)display_image->w, (uint32_t)display_image->h,
647                          (uint32_t)stride, color_filter, time_seconds);
648     }
649   }
650 
651   const image_t *ascii_input_image = filtered_image ? filtered_image : display_image;
652   uint64_t t_filter_end = time_get_ns();
653 
654   uint64_t t_convert_start = time_get_ns();
655   // Call the standard ASCII conversion using the filtered (or original) image
656   START_TIMER("ascii_convert_with_capabilities");
657   char *result = ascii_convert_with_capabilities(ascii_input_image, width, height, &caps_copy, preserve_aspect_ratio,
658                                                  stretch, ctx->palette_chars);
659   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, 3 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, 5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "ascii_convert_with_capabilities",
660                            "ASCII_CONVERT: Conversion complete (%.2f ms)");
661   uint64_t t_convert_end = time_get_ns();
662 
663   // Apply rainbow color filter to ANSI output by replacing RGB values
664   // This preserves character selection while applying the filter colors
665   if (result && color_filter == COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW) {
666     uint64_t t_color_replace_start = time_get_ns();
667     float time_seconds = (float)t_filter_start / (float)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
668 
669     char *rainbow_result = rainbow_replace_ansi_colors(result, time_seconds);
670     if (rainbow_result) {
671       SAFE_FREE(result);
672       result = rainbow_result;
673     }
674 
675     uint64_t t_color_replace_end = time_get_ns();
676     char color_replace_str[32];
677     time_pretty(t_color_replace_end - t_color_replace_start, -1, color_replace_str, sizeof(color_replace_str));
678     log_dev("COLOR_REPLACE: %s", color_replace_str);
679   }
680 
681   // Apply digital rain effect if enabled
682   if (result && ctx->digital_rain) {
683     uint64_t t_rain_start = time_get_ns();
684     uint64_t current_time_ns = t_rain_start;
685     float delta_time = (float)(current_time_ns - ctx->last_frame_time_ns) / (float)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
686     ctx->last_frame_time_ns = current_time_ns;
687 
688     // Update digital rain color from current filter (allows live filter changes)
689     color_filter_t current_filter = GET_OPTION(color_filter);
690     digital_rain_set_color_from_filter(ctx->digital_rain, current_filter);
691 
692     char *rain_result = digital_rain_apply(ctx->digital_rain, result, delta_time);
693     if (rain_result) {
694       SAFE_FREE(result);
695       result = rain_result;
696     }
697 
698     uint64_t t_rain_end = time_get_ns();
699     char rain_str[32];
700     time_pretty(t_rain_end - t_rain_start, -1, rain_str, sizeof(rain_str));
701     log_dev("DIGITAL_RAIN: Effect applied (%s)", rain_str);
702   }
703 
704   uint64_t t_cleanup_start = time_get_ns();
705   // Clean up flipped and filtered images if created
706   START_TIMER("ascii_convert_cleanup");
707   if (filtered_image) {
708     image_destroy(filtered_image);
709   }
710   if (flipped_image) {
711     image_destroy(flipped_image);
712   }
713   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, 3 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, 2 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "ascii_convert_cleanup",
714                            "ASCII_CONVERT_CLEANUP: Cleanup complete (%.2f ms)");
715   uint64_t t_cleanup_end = time_get_ns();
716 
717   // Log total breakdown with actual measured times
718   log_dev("CONVERT_TIMING: flip=%llu us, filter=%llu us, convert=%llu us, cleanup=%llu us, TOTAL=%llu us",
719           (t_flip_end - t_flip_start) / 1000, (t_filter_end - t_filter_start) / 1000,
720           (t_convert_end - t_convert_start) / 1000, (t_cleanup_end - t_cleanup_start) / 1000,
721           (t_cleanup_end - t_flip_start) / 1000);
722 
723   return result;
724 }

session_display_ctx_t * session_display_create (const
  session_display_config_t * config)


#include <src/common/session/display.h>


Create a new session display context.


Parameters


Config Module Display
  configuration (must not be NULL)


Returns


Pointer to display context, or NULL on failure


Creates and initializes a session display context with the
    specified configuration. Detects terminal capabilities and initializes
    palette.




Note


Call session_display_destroy() to free resources
  when done.



On failure, sets asciichat_errno with error details.




Create a new session display context.




Executes comprehensive terminal reset sequence for clean display
    state. Skips terminal control operations in snapshot mode.




Parameters


snapshot_mode Whether snapshot mode is
  enabled


Definition at line 131 of file
    src/common/session/display.c.


131                                                                                       {
132   // Auto-create config from command-line options if NULL
133   session_display_config_t auto_config = {0};
134   if (!config) {
135     auto_config.snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
136     auto_config.palette_type = GET_OPTION(palette_type);
137     auto_config.custom_palette = GET_OPTION(palette_custom_set) ? GET_OPTION(palette_custom) : NULL;
138     auto_config.color_mode = TERM_COLOR_AUTO;
139     config = &auto_config;
140   }
141 
142   // Check if we should exit before starting initialization
143   if (config->should_exit_callback && config->should_exit_callback(config->callback_data)) {
144     return NULL;
145   }
146 
147   // Allocate context
148   session_display_ctx_t *ctx = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(session_display_ctx_t), session_display_ctx_t *);
149 
150   // Store configuration
151   ctx->snapshot_mode = config->snapshot_mode;
152   ctx->palette_type = config->palette_type;
153   ctx->audio_playback_enabled = config->enable_audio_playback;
154   ctx->audio_ctx = config->audio_ctx;
155   ctx->render_fps = config->render_fps;
156   atomic_store_bool(&ctx->first_frame, true);
157   atomic_store_bool(&ctx->keyboard_help_active, false);
158 
159   // Initialize FPS counter
160   ctx->fps_counter = fps_counter_create();
161 
162   // Get TTY info for direct terminal access
163   ctx->tty_info = get_current_tty();
164 
165   // Determine if we have a valid TTY
166   // Check if stdout is a TTY, not just any fd (stdin/stderr could be TTY while stdout is piped).
167   // If stdout is piped/redirected, never perform terminal operations regardless of other fds.
168   if (ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) {
169     ctx->has_tty = (platform_isatty(ctx->tty_info.fd) != 0) && terminal_is_stdout_tty();
170   }
171 
172   // In piped mode, force all logs to stderr to prevent frame data corruption
173   if (terminal_should_force_stderr()) {
174     log_set_force_stderr(true);
175     // Disable buffering for stdout to ensure frames appear immediately
176     // Each frame ends with newline, so unbuffered output is critical for real-time display
177     (void)setvbuf(stdout, NULL, _IONBF, 0);
178   }
179 
180   // Also disable buffering for TTY mode to ensure smooth 40+ FPS animation
181   setvbuf(stdout, NULL, _IONBF, 0);
182 
183   // Detect terminal capabilities
184   ctx->caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
185 
186   // Set wants_padding based on terminal output mode
187   // Enable padding for all rendering modes (including snapshot) to center output
188   // The padding adds newlines at the top, which is useful for visual centering
189   bool is_snapshot_mode = config->snapshot_mode;
190   ctx->caps.wants_padding = true;
191 
192   // Calculate pad_height: when padding is enabled, add top padding for centering
193   // Halfblock mode uses 2 source rows per output row, so padding is halved
194   if (ctx->caps.wants_padding) {
195     ctx->caps.pad_height = 5; // Halfblock needs half the padding (10 / 2 = 5)
196   } else {
197     ctx->caps.pad_height = 0;
198   }
199 
200   log_debug("Padding mode: wants_padding=%d (snapshot=%d, has_tty=%d, stdin_tty=%d, stdout_tty=%d), pad_height=%zu",
201             ctx->caps.wants_padding, is_snapshot_mode, ctx->has_tty, terminal_is_stdin_tty(), terminal_is_stdout_tty(),
202             ctx->caps.pad_height);
203 
204   // Apply color mode override if specified
205   if (config->color_mode != TERM_COLOR_AUTO) {
206     ctx->caps.color_level = config->color_mode;
207   }
208 
209   // Apply command-line overrides
210   ctx->caps = apply_color_mode_override(ctx->caps);
211 
212   // Initialize palette
213   int palette_result = initialize_client_palette(config->palette_type, config->custom_palette, ctx->palette_chars,
214                                                  &ctx->palette_len, ctx->luminance_palette);
215   if (palette_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
216     log_warn("Failed to initialize palette, using default");
217     // Fall back to standard palette
218     (void)initialize_client_palette(PALETTE_STANDARD, NULL, ctx->palette_chars, &ctx->palette_len,
219                                     ctx->luminance_palette);
220   }
221 
222   // Pre-warm UTF-8 palette cache during initialization (not during rendering)
223   // This is expensive on first use (creates lookup tables), so warm it up now to avoid frame lag
224   // This function is imported from lib/video/simd/common.h
225   (void)get_utf8_palette_cache((const char *)ctx->palette_chars);
226   log_debug("UTF-8 palette cache pre-warmed during display initialization");
227 
228   // Initialize ANSI fast lookup tables based on terminal capabilities
229   if (ctx->caps.color_level == TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR) {
230     ansi_fast_init();
231   } else if (ctx->caps.color_level == TERM_COLOR_256) {
232     ansi_fast_init_256color();
233   } else if (ctx->caps.color_level == TERM_COLOR_16) {
234     ansi_fast_init_16color();
235   }
236   // TERM_COLOR_MONO requires no init
237 
238   // Initialize ASCII output system if we have a TTY
239   if (ctx->has_tty && ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) {
240     ascii_write_init(ctx->tty_info.fd, false);
241   }
242 
243   // Initialize digital rain effect if enabled
244   if (GET_OPTION(matrix_rain)) {
245     // Get terminal dimensions for grid size
246     unsigned short int width_us = terminal_get_effective_width();
247     unsigned short int height_us = terminal_get_effective_height();
248 
249     int width = (int)width_us;
250     int height = (int)height_us;
251 
252     ctx->digital_rain = digital_rain_init(width, height);
253     if (ctx->digital_rain) {
254       // Set color from color filter if active
255       color_filter_t filter = GET_OPTION(color_filter);
256       digital_rain_set_color_from_filter(ctx->digital_rain, filter);
257       log_info("Digital rain effect enabled: %dx%d grid", width, height);
258     } else {
259       log_warn("Failed to initialize digital rain effect");
260     }
261     ctx->last_frame_time_ns = time_get_ns();
262   }
263 
264 #ifndef _WIN32
265   // Initialize render-file if enabled (FFmpeg encodes to stdout when "-" is specified)
266   const char *render_file_opt = GET_OPTION(render_file);
267   log_info("DISPLAY_CREATE: render-file opt='%s' (len=%zu), will initialize=%s",
268            render_file_opt ? render_file_opt : "(null)", render_file_opt ? strlen(render_file_opt) : 0,
269            (render_file_opt && strlen(render_file_opt) > 0) ? "YES" : "NO");
270   if (render_file_opt && strlen(render_file_opt) > 0) {
271     int width = (int)GET_OPTION(width);
272     int height = (int)GET_OPTION(height);
273     log_info("render-file: Creating encoder with cols=%d, rows=%d", width, height);
274     // Determine encoder FPS: in snapshot mode, use actual capture rate for correct duration;
275     // in normal mode, use configured fps option
276     uint32_t encoder_fps = 0;
277     if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && config->render_fps > 0) {
278       // Snapshot mode with known capture rate - use capture rate for correct duration
279       encoder_fps = config->render_fps;
280       log_debug("render-file: SNAPSHOT MODE - using capture rate FPS=%u (snapshot_delay=%.2f sec)", encoder_fps,
281                 GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay));
282     } else if (config->render_fps > 0) {
283       // Normal mode with known capture rate - use it
284       encoder_fps = config->render_fps;
285       log_debug("render-file: Using capture rate FPS=%u for encoding", encoder_fps);
286     } else {
287       // No capture rate available - use option default
288       encoder_fps = (uint32_t)GET_OPTION(fps);
289       if (encoder_fps == 0)
290         encoder_fps = 60;
291       log_debug("render-file: Using option FPS=%u (no capture rate available)", encoder_fps);
292     }
293     log_info("render-file: Final encoder FPS=%u (config_render_fps=%u, option_fps=%u)", encoder_fps, config->render_fps,
294              (uint32_t)GET_OPTION(fps));
295     log_info("render-file: Calling render_file_create with path='%s', %dx%d, fps=%d", render_file_opt, width, height,
296              encoder_fps);
297     asciichat_error_t rf_err = render_file_create(render_file_opt, width, height, (int)encoder_fps,
298                                                   GET_OPTION(render_theme), &ctx->render_file);
299     if (rf_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
300       log_error("render-file: FAILED to create with error %d (ctx->render_file=%p)", rf_err, (void *)ctx->render_file);
301     } else {
302       log_info("render-file: SUCCESS - encoder initialized, ctx->render_file=%p", (void *)ctx->render_file);
303       // Set audio sources if render_file_set_audio_source is available
304       if (ctx->render_file && config->render_file_audio_source) {
305         render_file_set_audio_source((render_file_ctx_t *)ctx->render_file, config->render_file_audio_source, NULL);
306         log_debug("render-file: audio source set (media_source=%p)", config->render_file_audio_source);
307       }
308       if (ctx->render_file && config->render_file_audio_capture_rb) {
309         render_file_set_audio_source((render_file_ctx_t *)ctx->render_file, NULL, config->render_file_audio_capture_rb);
310         log_debug("render-file: audio capture ring buffer set");
311       }
312     }
313   } else {
314     log_info("render-file: NOT initializing (render_file_opt is %s)", render_file_opt ? "empty" : "NULL");
315   }
316 #endif
317 
318   ctx->initialized = true;
319   return ctx;
320 }

void
  session_display_destroy (session_display_ctx_t * ctx)


#include <src/common/session/display.h>


Destroy session display context and free resources.


Parameters


ctx Display context to destroy (can be NULL)


Cleans up the display context, restores terminal state, and
    releases all resources. Safe to call with NULL.


Definition at line 322 of file
    src/common/session/display.c.


322                                                          {
323   if (!ctx) {
324     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session display context is NULL");
325     return;
326   }
327 
328   // Cleanup ASCII rendering if we had a TTY
329   // Don't reset terminal in snapshot mode to preserve the rendered output
330   if (ctx->has_tty && ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) {
331     ascii_write_destroy(ctx->tty_info.fd, !ctx->snapshot_mode);
332   }
333 
334   // Close the controlling terminal if we opened it
335   if (ctx->tty_info.owns_fd && ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) {
336     (void)platform_close(ctx->tty_info.fd);
337     ctx->tty_info.fd = -1;
338     ctx->tty_info.owns_fd = false;
339   }
340 
341   // Cleanup digital rain effect
342   if (ctx->digital_rain) {
343     digital_rain_destroy(ctx->digital_rain);
344     ctx->digital_rain = NULL;
345   }
346 
347   // Cleanup FPS counter
348   if (ctx->fps_counter) {
349     fps_counter_destroy(ctx->fps_counter);
350     ctx->fps_counter = NULL;
351   }
352 
353 #ifndef _WIN32
354   // Cleanup render-file if active
355   if (ctx->render_file) {
356     render_file_destroy(ctx->render_file);
357     ctx->render_file = NULL;
358   }
359 #endif
360 
361   ctx->initialized = false;
362 
363   // Clear the cached display context in splash state to prevent use-after-free
364   // when worker threads try to access the display after it's been freed
365   splash_clear_display_context();
366 
367   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
368 }






References ascii_write_destroy(),

Encode frame to render-file (FFmpeg only, no terminal output).


Parameters


ctx Display context (must not be NULL)

942   (void)ctx; (void)image; (void)captured_ns;
943   return; // render-file not supported on Windows
944 #else
945   static int call_count = 0;
946   if (call_count++ < 5) {
947     log_info("session_display_encode_frame: CALLED (ctx=%p, image=%p, captured_ns=%llu, ctx->render_file=%p)",
948              (void *)ctx, (void *)image, (unsigned long long)captured_ns, ctx ? (void *)ctx->render_file : NULL);
949   }
950 
951   if (!ctx || !ctx->initialized) {
952     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Display context is NULL or uninitialized");
953     return;
954   }
955 
956   if (!image) {
957     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Image is NULL");
958     return;
959   }
960 
961   // Only encode if render_file is active
962   if (!ctx->render_file) {
963     log_warn_every(10 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, "session_display_encode_frame: render_file is NULL, skipping encode");
964     return;
965   }
966 
967   // Convert image to ASCII
968   char *ascii = session_display_convert_to_ascii(ctx, image);
969   if (!ascii) {
970     log_warn("session_display_encode_frame: Failed to convert image to ASCII");
971     return;
972   }
973 
974   // Write ASCII to render-file encoder
975 #ifndef _WIN32
976   asciichat_error_t fe = render_file_write_frame(ctx->render_file, ascii, captured_ns);
977   if (fe != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
978     log_warn_every(5 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, "render-file: encode failed (%s)", asciichat_error_string(fe));
979   }
980 #endif
981 
982   SAFE_FREE(ascii);
983 #endif // !_WIN32
984 }

void
  session_display_render_fps_overlay (session_display_ctx_t *
  ctx)


#include <src/common/session/display.h>


Render FPS counter overlay in top-right corner.


Parameters


ctx Display context (must not be NULL)


Displays the current FPS value as a reverse-video overlay in the
    top-right corner of the terminal. Only renders in TTY mode. Safe to call
    when fps_counter is NULL.


Definition at line 1006 of file
    src/common/session/display.c.


1006                                                                     {
1007   if (!ctx || !ctx->fps_counter || !ctx->initialized) {
1008     return;
1009   }
1010 
1011   // Only render overlay in TTY mode
1012   if (!ctx->has_tty) {
1013     return;
1014   }
1015 
1016   // Get current FPS value from counter
1017   float fps = fps_counter_get(ctx->fps_counter);
1018 
1019   // Get terminal width for right-alignment
1020   int term_cols = (int)terminal_get_effective_width();
1021 
1022   // Position at row 1, right side of screen
1023   // Column = term_cols - 7 (to fit "FPS:144" which is 7 chars)
1024   // Use reverse video (\033[7m) for visibility, then reset (\033[0m)
1025   char overlay[64];
1026   int overlay_len = snprintf(overlay, sizeof(overlay), "\033[1;%dH\033[7mFPS:%3.0f\033[0m", term_cols - 6, fps);
1027 
1028   if (overlay_len > 0 && overlay_len < (int)sizeof(overlay)) {
1029     // Write overlay to TTY
1030     int fd = (ctx->has_tty && ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) ? ctx->tty_info.fd : STDOUT_FILENO;
1031     (void)platform_write_all(fd, overlay, overlay_len);
1032 
1033     // Flush to ensure overlay appears immediately
1034     (void)terminal_flush(fd);
1035   }
1036 }
