753     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ASCII data is NULL");
754     return;
755   }
756 
757   // Re-evaluate color_level on every frame to pick up live color_mode changes
758   terminal_color_mode_t color_mode = (terminal_color_mode_t)GET_OPTION(color_mode);
759   if (color_mode != TERM_COLOR_AUTO) {
760     ctx->caps.color_level = color_mode;
761   }
762 
763   // Suppress frame rendering when help screen is active
764   if (atomic_load_bool(&ctx->keyboard_help_active)) {
765     return;
766   }
767 
768   // Calculate frame length
769   size_t frame_len = strnlen(ascii, 1024 * 1024); // Max 1MB frame
770   if (frame_len == 0) {
771     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ASCII data is empty");
772     return;
773   }
774 
775   // Apply digital rain effect if enabled
776   char *display_frame = (char *)ascii;
777   char *rain_result = NULL;
778   if (ctx->digital_rain) {
779     uint64_t t_rain_start = time_get_ns();
780     uint64_t current_time_ns = t_rain_start;
781     float delta_time = (float)(current_time_ns - ctx->last_frame_time_ns) / (float)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
782     ctx->last_frame_time_ns = current_time_ns;
783 
784     // Update digital rain color from current filter
785     color_filter_t current_filter = GET_OPTION(color_filter);
786     digital_rain_set_color_from_filter(ctx->digital_rain, current_filter);
787 
788     rain_result = digital_rain_apply(ctx->digital_rain, (char *)ascii, delta_time);
789     if (rain_result) {
790       display_frame = rain_result;
791       frame_len = strnlen(rain_result, 1024 * 1024);
792 
793       uint64_t t_rain_end = time_get_ns();
794       char rain_str[32];
795       time_pretty(t_rain_end - t_rain_start, -1, rain_str, sizeof(rain_str));
796       log_info("DIGITAL_RAIN (write_ascii): Effect applied (%s)", rain_str);
797     }
798   }
799 
800   // Handle first frame - perform initial terminal reset and splash cleanup
801   bool was_first_frame = atomic_exchange_bool(&ctx->first_frame, false);
802   if (was_first_frame) {
803     // Stop splash screen when first frame is ready
804     splash_intro_done();
805 
806     // Perform initial terminal reset
807     if (ctx->has_tty
808 #ifndef _WIN32
809         && !ctx->render_file
810 #endif
811     ) {
812       (void)terminal_reset(STDOUT_FILENO);
813       (void)terminal_clear_screen();
814       (void)terminal_cursor_home(STDOUT_FILENO);
815       (void)terminal_clear_scrollback(STDOUT_FILENO);
816       (void)terminal_cursor_show();
817       if (!ctx->snapshot_mode) {
818         (void)terminal_cursor_hide();
819       }
820       (void)terminal_flush(STDOUT_FILENO);
821     }
822   }
823 
824   // Output routing logic
825   bool use_tty_control = ctx->has_tty && (!ctx->snapshot_mode || terminal_is_interactive());
826 
827   START_TIMER("frame_write");
828 
829   // Increment shared frame counter for both client and host rendering
830   // This metric is used by debug scripts to measure FPS across all modes
831   static atomic_t g_display_frames_rendered = {0};
832   int frame_number = atomic_fetch_add_u64(&g_display_frames_rendered, 1) + 1;
833   log_info("[FRAMES_RENDERED_TOTAL] %d", frame_number);
834 
835   if (use_tty_control) {
836     // TTY mode: Buffer cursor control + frame data together for atomic frame display
837     const char *cursor_home_sequence = "\033[H\033[3J"; // 7 bytes total
838     size_t cursor_seq_len = 7;
839     size_t total_size = cursor_seq_len + frame_len;
840 
841     char *frame_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(total_size, char *);
842     if (frame_buffer) {
843       memcpy(frame_buffer, cursor_home_sequence, cursor_seq_len);
844       memcpy(frame_buffer + cursor_seq_len, display_frame, frame_len);
845 
846       log_debug("FRAME_WRITE_TTY: Writing %zu bytes (cursor=%zu + frame=%zu) to stdout", total_size, cursor_seq_len,
847                 frame_len);
848       ssize_t written = platform_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, frame_buffer, total_size);
849       log_debug("FRAME_WRITE_TTY: Wrote %zd bytes (requested %zu)", written, total_size);
850 
851       // Start snapshot timer on first ASCII frame rendered
852       if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && !g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered) {
853         g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered = true;
854         g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns = time_get_ns();
855         log_info("SNAPSHOT: FIRST ASCII FRAME RENDERED (write_ascii) - Timer started");
856       }
857 
858       // Tick FPS counter
859       if (ctx->fps_counter) {
860         fps_counter_tick(ctx->fps_counter);
861       }
862 
863       SAFE_FREE(frame_buffer);
864     }
865 
866     // Flush buffers
867     log_debug("FRAME_FLUSH: Flushing stdout");
868     (void)fflush(stdout);
869     int flush_fd = (ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) ? ctx->tty_info.fd : STDOUT_FILENO;
870     (void)terminal_flush(flush_fd);
871 
872     // Render FPS counter overlay if enabled
873     if (ctx->fps_counter && GET_OPTION(fps_counter)) {
874       session_display_render_fps_overlay(ctx);
875     }
876   } else if (terminal_is_interactive()) {
877     // Piped to interactive terminal: output ASCII frames with newline
878     char *write_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(frame_len + 1, char *);
879     if (write_buf) {
880       memcpy(write_buf, display_frame, frame_len);
881       write_buf[frame_len] = '\n';
882       (void)platform_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, write_buf, frame_len + 1);
883       SAFE_FREE(write_buf);
884     }
885 
886     // Flush buffers
887     (void)fflush(stdout);
888     int flush_fd = (ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) ? ctx->tty_info.fd : STDOUT_FILENO;
889     (void)terminal_flush(flush_fd);
890   } else {
891     // Non-interactive piped output
892     // BUT: Don't write ASCII frames to stdout if render_file is using stdout (--render-file="-")
893     if (true
894 #ifndef _WIN32
895         && !ctx->render_file
896 #endif
897     ) {
898       // No render-file, safe to write ASCII frames to stdout
899       char *write_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(frame_len + 1, char *);
900       if (write_buf) {
901         memcpy(write_buf, display_frame, frame_len);
902         write_buf[frame_len] = '\n';
903         (void)platform_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, write_buf, frame_len + 1);
904 
905         // Start snapshot timer on first ASCII frame rendered
906         if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && !g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered) {
907           g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered = true;
908           g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns = time_get_ns();
909           log_info("SNAPSHOT: FIRST ASCII FRAME RENDERED (write_ascii piped) - Timer started");
910         }
911 
912         SAFE_FREE(write_buf);
913       }
914 
915       // Flush buffers (skip terminal_flush on pipes to avoid blocking in snapshot mode)
916       (void)fflush(stdout);
917       if (!GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) || ctx->has_tty) {
918         int flush_fd = (ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) ? ctx->tty_info.fd : STDOUT_FILENO;
919         (void)terminal_flush(flush_fd);
920       }
921     } else {
922       // render_file is using stdout: skip ASCII output to avoid mixing with video
923       if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && !g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered) {
924         g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered = true;
925         g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns = time_get_ns();
926         log_info("SNAPSHOT: FIRST FRAME RENDERED (render_file piped) - Timer started");
927       }
928     }
929   }
930 
931   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, 3 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, 5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "frame_write",
932                            "FRAME_WRITE: Write and flush complete (%.2f ms)");

934   // Clean up digital rain result if allocated

asciichat_error_t session_display_write_audio
  (session_display_ctx_t * ctx, const float * buffer, size_t
  num_samples)


#include <src/common/session/display.h>


Write audio samples to playback buffer.


Parameters


ctx Display context (must not be NULL)


buffer Audio samples to write (must not be NULL)


num_samples Number of samples to write


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Writes audio samples to the playback ring buffer for playback
    through speakers. Used in mirror mode to play file audio or other audio
    sources.


Definition at line 1095 of file
    src/common/session/display.c.


1095                                                                                                                    {
1096   if (!ctx || !ctx->initialized || !buffer || num_samples == 0) {
1097     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: ctx=%p, buffer=%p, num_samples=%zu", ctx, buffer,
1098                      num_samples);
1099   }
1100 
1101   if (!ctx->audio_playback_enabled || !ctx->audio_ctx) {
1102     // Audio not enabled, but not an error - just skip silently
1103     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1104   }
1105 
1106   // For mirror mode with local files: write samples directly without jitter buffering
1107   // The jitter buffer is designed for network scenarios with irregular packet arrivals
1108   // For local playback, we just want raw samples flowing to the speakers
1109   audio_context_t *audio_ctx = (audio_context_t *)ctx->audio_ctx;
1110   if (audio_ctx && audio_ctx->playback_buffer) {
1111     audio_ring_buffer_t *rb = audio_ctx->playback_buffer;
1112 
1113     // Simple direct write: append samples to ring buffer without network-oriented complexity
1114     uint32_t write_idx = (uint32_t)atomic_load_u64(&rb->write_index);
1115     uint32_t read_idx = (uint32_t)atomic_load_u64(&rb->read_index);
1116 
1117     // Calculate available space in ring buffer
1118     uint32_t available = (read_idx - write_idx - 1) & (AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - 1);
1119 
1120     if ((uint32_t)num_samples > available) {
1121       // Buffer full - just skip this write to avoid distortion from overwriting old audio
1122       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1123     }
1124 
1125     // Direct memcpy to ring buffer, handling wrap-around
1126     uint32_t space_before_wrap = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - write_idx;
1127     if ((uint32_t)num_samples <= space_before_wrap) {
1128       memcpy(&rb->data[write_idx], buffer, num_samples * sizeof(float));
1129     } else {
1130       // Split write: first part to end of buffer, second part wraps to beginning
1131       uint32_t first_part = space_before_wrap;
1132       uint32_t second_part = num_samples - first_part;
1133       memcpy(&rb->data[write_idx], buffer, first_part * sizeof(float));
1134       memcpy(&rb->data[0], &buffer[first_part], second_part * sizeof(float));
1135     }
1136 
1137     // Update write index atomically
1138     atomic_store_u64(&rb->write_index, (write_idx + num_samples) % AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE);
1139 
1140     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1141   }
1142 
1143   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1144 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_u64(),

    session_display_ctx::audio_playback_enabled,
    AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE, audio_ring_buffer::data,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, session_display_ctx::initialized,
    audio_context_t::playback_buffer,
    audio_ring_buffer::read_index, SET_ERRNO, and
    audio_ring_buffer::write_index.

992   int fd = -1;

void
  session_handle_keyboard_input (session_capture_ctx_t * capture,
  session_display_ctx_t * display, keyboard_key_t key)


#include
    <src/common/session/keyboard_handler.h>


Handle keyboard input in a session.


Parameters


capture Capture context (may be NULL for
  client-only mode)


display Display context (may be NULL if not needed)


key Keyboard key code from keyboard_key_t enumeration


Processes keyboard input and performs appropriate session actions.
    Supports both mirror mode (local capture) and client mode (network
    rendering).




Keyboard Controls:


    
  
  • '?' key: Toggle help screen on/off (all modes)
    • 
  
  • Left Arrow: Seek -30 seconds (file/URL sources only)
    • 
  
  • Right Arrow: Seek +30 seconds (file/URL sources only)
    • 
  
  • Up Arrow: Increase volume +10% (all modes with audio)
    • 
  
  • Down Arrow: Decrease volume -10% (all modes with audio)
    • 
  
  • Spacebar: Play/pause toggle (file/URL sources only)
    • 
  
  • 'c' key: Cycle through color modes (mono → 16 → 256
      → truecolor)
    • 
  
  • 'm' key: Toggle mute (remembers previous volume for unmute)
    • 
  
  • 'x' key: Flip webcam horizontally (mirroring)
    • 
  
  • 'y' key: Flip webcam vertically
    • 



Source-specific behavior:


    
  
  • Help screen: Works in all modes (toggles with '?')
    • 
  
  • Seek/pause controls: Only work with file and URL sources (not webcam)
    • 
  
  • Volume/mute: Work with any audio-capable source
    • 
  
  • Color cycling: Works with all rendering modes
    • 
  
  • Flip: Works with all capture sources (webcam, media files, etc.)
    • 



Audio volume:


    
  
  • Volume range: [0.0, 1.0] (0% = silent, 100% = maximum/normal)
    • 
  
  • Default volume: 1.0 (100%)
    • 
  
  • Mute state: Remembers the previous non-zero volume when toggled back
    on
    • 
  
  • Unrecognized keys are silently ignored (no error returned)
    • 



Thread safety:


    
  
  • Safe to call from render thread (video/audio threads)
    • 
  
  • Safe to call concurrently from multiple render threads
    • 
  
  • Uses RCU-protected options for atomic updates
    • 
  
  • No explicit locking required (option system handles synchronization)
    • 
  
  • Does not block waiting for I/O or locks
    • 



Error handling:


    
  
  • Always succeeds (void return)
    • 
  
  • Invalid or unrecognized keys are silently ignored
    • 
  
  • If capture is NULL (client-only mode), seek/pause requests are silently
      ignored
    • 
  
  • If display is NULL (non-TTY mode), help screen toggle is silently
    ignored
    • 
  
  • If audio system is unavailable, volume changes are silently ignored
    • 



Parameters


capture NULL for client-only mode (network
  rendering), non-NULL for local capture


display Display context for help screen toggle (may be NULL)


key Valid key code from keyboard_key_t enum (0-127 for ASCII, special key
  codes)


Note


Safe to call without prior keyboard_init() (silently
  handles invalid keys)



In client-only mode, capture is NULL (seek/pause unavailable,
    other controls work)




Volume adjustments are immediate without audible artifacts




Mute persists across multiple mute toggles with volume memory




Definition at line 104 of file
  keyboard_handler.c.


104                                                                                                                        {
105   // Debug: log all key codes to help identify unknown keys
106   if (key != KEY_NONE) {
107     log_info("KEYBOARD INPUT: code=%d (0x%02x) char='%c'", key, key, (key >= 32 && key < 127) ? key : '?');
108   }
109 
110   switch ((int)key) {
111   // ===== HELP SCREEN TOGGLE =====
112   case KEY_QUESTION: {
113     log_info("✓✓✓ USER PRESSED ? KEY - HELP SCREEN TOGGLE ✓✓✓");
114     if (display) {
115       log_info("Toggling help screen (display=%p)", (void *)display);
116       keyboard_help_toggle(display);
117       // Render help screen immediately so user sees it
118       keyboard_help_render(display);
119       log_info("✓ Help screen toggle complete");
120     } else {
121       log_error("ERROR: Cannot toggle help - display context is NULL");
122     }
123     break;
124   }
125 
126   // ===== HELP SCREEN CLOSE / QUIT =====
127   case KEY_ESCAPE: {
128     if (display && keyboard_help_is_active(display)) {
129       // Close help screen if it's active
130       keyboard_help_toggle(display);
131       terminal_clear_screen();
132     } else {
133       // If help screen is not active, quit the app (like Ctrl-C)
134       // The signal handler will gracefully shutdown all modes (client, server, mirror, etc.)
135       raise(SIGINT);
136     }
137     break;
138   }
139 
140   // ===== SEEK CONTROLS (file sources only) =====
141   case KEY_LEFT: { // Seek backward 30 seconds
142     if (capture) {
143       media_source_t *source = (media_source_t *)session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
144       if (source && media_source_get_type(source) == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE) {
145         double current_pos = media_source_get_position(source);
146         if (current_pos >= 0.0) {
147           double new_pos = current_pos - 30.0;
148           if (new_pos < 0.0) {
149             new_pos = 0.0;
150           }
151           asciichat_error_t err = media_source_seek(source, new_pos);
152           if (err == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
153             log_info("Seeked backward to %.1f seconds", new_pos);
154           }
155           void *audio_ctx = session_capture_get_audio_context(capture);
156           if (audio_ctx) {
157             audio_flush_playback_buffers((audio_context_t *)audio_ctx);
158           }
159         }
160       }
161     }
162     break;
163   }
164 
165   case KEY_RIGHT: { // Seek forward 30 seconds
166     if (capture) {
167       media_source_t *source = (media_source_t *)session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
168       if (source && media_source_get_type(source) == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE) {
169         double current_pos = media_source_get_position(source);
170         double duration = media_source_get_duration(source);
171         if (current_pos >= 0.0) {
172           double new_pos = current_pos + 30.0;
173           // Clamp to duration if known
174           if (duration > 0.0 && new_pos > duration) {
175             new_pos = duration;
176           }
177           asciichat_error_t err = media_source_seek(source, new_pos);
178           if (err == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
179             log_info("Seeked forward to %.1f seconds", new_pos);
180           }
181           void *audio_ctx = session_capture_get_audio_context(capture);
182           if (audio_ctx) {
183             audio_flush_playback_buffers((audio_context_t *)audio_ctx);
184           }
185         }
186       }
187     }
188     break;
189   }
190 
191   // ===== VOLUME CONTROLS =====
192   case KEY_DOWN: { // Decrease volume 10%
193     double current_volume = GET_OPTION(speakers_volume);
194     double new_volume = clamp_volume(current_volume - 0.1);
195     options_set_double("speakers_volume", new_volume);
196     double verify_volume = GET_OPTION(speakers_volume);
197     log_info("Volume DOWN: %.0f%% → %.0f%% (verified: %.0f%%)", current_volume * 100.0, new_volume * 100.0,
198              verify_volume * 100.0);
199     break;
200   }
201 
202   case KEY_UP: { // Increase volume 10%
203     double current_volume = GET_OPTION(speakers_volume);
204     double new_volume = clamp_volume(current_volume + 0.1);
205     options_set_double("speakers_volume", new_volume);
206     double verify_volume = GET_OPTION(speakers_volume);
207     log_info("Volume UP: %.0f%% → %.0f%% (verified: %.0f%%)", current_volume * 100.0, new_volume * 100.0,
208              verify_volume * 100.0);
209     break;
210   }
211 
212   // ===== PLAY/PAUSE CONTROL =====
213   case KEY_SPACE: { // Toggle play/pause
214     if (capture) {
215       media_source_t *source = (media_source_t *)session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
216       if (source && media_source_get_type(source) == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE) {
217         media_source_toggle_pause(source);
218         if (media_source_is_paused(source)) {
219           log_info("Paused");
220         } else {
221           log_info("Playing");
222         }
223       }
224     }
225     break;
226   }
227 
228   // ===== COLOR MODE CONTROL =====
229   case KEY_C:
230   case 'C': {
231     int current_mode = (int)GET_OPTION(color_mode);
232     int next_mode = next_color_mode(current_mode);
233     options_set_int("color_mode", next_mode);
234 
235     const char *mode_names[] = {"Mono", "16-color", "256-color", "Truecolor"};
236     if (next_mode >= 0 && next_mode <= 3) {
237       log_info("Color mode: %s", mode_names[next_mode]);
238     }
239     break;
240   }
241 
242   // ===== MUTE CONTROL =====
243   case KEY_M:
244   case 'M': {
245     double current_volume = GET_OPTION(speakers_volume);
246     log_debug("Mute toggle: current_volume=%.2f, g_mute_saved_volume=%.2f, threshold=0.01", current_volume,
247               g_mute_saved_volume);
248 
249     if (current_volume > 0.01) { // If not already muted
250       // Save current volume and mute
251       g_mute_saved_volume = current_volume;
252       options_set_double("speakers_volume", 0.0);
253       double verify = GET_OPTION(speakers_volume);
254       log_info("Muted: saved %.0f%%, set to 0%% (verified: %.2f)", g_mute_saved_volume * 100.0, verify);
255     } else {
256       // Restore previous volume
257       double restore_volume = g_mute_saved_volume > 0.0 ? g_mute_saved_volume : 0.5;
258       options_set_double("speakers_volume", restore_volume);
259       double verify = GET_OPTION(speakers_volume);
260       log_info("Unmuted: restored %.0f%% (verified: %.2f)", restore_volume * 100.0, verify);
261     }
262     break;
263   }
264 
265   // ===== RENDER MODE CONTROL =====
266   case KEY_R:
267   case 'R': {
268     int current_mode = (int)GET_OPTION(render_mode);
269     int next_mode = next_render_mode(current_mode);
270     options_set_int("render_mode", next_mode);
271 
272     const char *mode_names[] = {"Foreground", "Background", "Half-block"};
273     if (next_mode >= 0 && next_mode <= 2) {
274       log_info("Render mode: %s", mode_names[next_mode]);
275     }
276     break;
277   }
278 
279   // ===== COLOR FILTER CONTROL =====
280   case KEY_F:
281   case 'F': {
282     int current_filter = (int)GET_OPTION(color_filter);
283     int next_filter = next_color_filter(current_filter);
284     options_set_int("color_filter", next_filter);
285 
286     if (next_filter >= 0 && next_filter < (int)COLOR_FILTER_COUNT) {
287       log_info("Color filter: %s", COLOR_FILTER_NAMES[next_filter]);
288     }
289     break;
290   }
291 
292   // ===== HORIZONTAL FLIP CONTROL =====
293   case 'X':
294   case 'x': {
295     log_info("✓ X KEY PRESSED - handling horizontal flip");
296     bool current_flip_x = (bool)GET_OPTION(flip_x);
297     options_set_bool("flip_x", !current_flip_x);
298     log_info("Horizontal flip: %s (was: %s)", !current_flip_x ? "enabled" : "disabled",
299              current_flip_x ? "enabled" : "disabled");
300     break;
301   }
302 
303   // ===== VERTICAL FLIP CONTROL =====
304   case 'Y':
305   case 'y': {
306     bool current_flip_y = (bool)GET_OPTION(flip_y);
307     options_set_bool("flip_y", !current_flip_y);
308     log_info("Vertical flip: %s", !current_flip_y ? "enabled" : "disabled");
309     break;
310   }
311 
312   // ===== MATRIX RAIN EFFECT CONTROL =====
313   case KEY_0: {
314     bool current_matrix = (bool)GET_OPTION(matrix_rain);
315     options_set_bool("matrix_rain", !current_matrix);
316     log_info("Matrix rain effect: %s", !current_matrix ? "enabled" : "disabled");
317     break;
318   }
319 
320   // ===== FPS COUNTER TOGGLE =====
321   case KEY_MINUS: {
322     bool current = (bool)GET_OPTION(fps_counter);
323     options_set_bool("fps_counter", !current);
324     log_info("FPS counter: %s", !current ? "enabled" : "disabled");
325     break;
326   }
327 
328   // ===== LOCK DEBUG (debug builds only) =====
329 #ifndef NDEBUG
330   case KEY_BACKTICK: {
331     debug_sync_trigger_print();
332     log_debug("Lock state dump triggered via backtick key");
333     break;
334   }
335 #endif
336 
337   default:
338     // Unknown key - log it for debugging
339     if (key != KEY_NONE) {
340       log_info("UNKNOWN KEY: code=%d (0x%02x) char='%c'", key, key, (key >= 32 && key < 127) ? key : '?');
341     }
342     break;
343   }
344 
345   // Re-render help screen if it's active to show updated settings
346   if (display && keyboard_help_is_active(display)) {
347     keyboard_help_render(display);
348   }
349 }

uint32_t session_host_add_client (session_host_t *
  host, socket_t socket, const char * ip, int port)


#include <src/common/session/host.h>


Add a client from an accepted socket.


Parameters


host Host handle (must not be NULL)


socket Accepted client socket


ip Client IP address


port Client port


Returns


Assigned client ID on success, 0 on failure


Registers a new client from an accepted socket connection. This is
    typically called internally by the accept loop, but can be used for
  testing.




Note


on_client_join callback is invoked on successful
  registration.


Definition at line 964 of file host.c.


964                                                                                                   {
965   if (!host || !host->initialized) {
966     return 0;
967   }
968 
969   mutex_lock(&host->clients_mutex);
970 
971   // Check if we have room
972   if (host->client_count >= host->max_clients) {
973     mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
974     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_SESSION_FULL, "Maximum clients reached");
975     return 0;
976   }
977 
978   // Find empty slot
979   for (int i = 0; i < host->max_clients; i++) {
980     if (!host->clients[i].active) {
981       host->clients[i].participant_type = PARTICIPANT_TYPE_NETWORK;
982       host->clients[i].client_id = host->next_client_id++;
983       host->clients[i].socket = socket;
984       if (ip) {
985         SAFE_STRNCPY(host->clients[i].ip_address, ip, sizeof(host->clients[i].ip_address));
986       }
987       host->clients[i].port = port;
988       host->clients[i].active = true;
989       host->clients[i].video_active = false;
990       host->clients[i].audio_active = false;
991       host->clients[i].connected_at = (uint64_t)time(NULL);
992       host->clients[i].transport = NULL; // No alternative transport initially
993 
994       // Allocate media buffers
995       host->clients[i].incoming_video = image_new(480, 270);                        // Network-optimal size (HD preview)
996       host->clients[i].incoming_audio = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(float), 960 * 10); // ~200ms buffer @ 48kHz
997 
998       if (!host->clients[i].incoming_video || !host->clients[i].incoming_audio) {
999         // Cleanup on allocation failure
1000         if (host->clients[i].incoming_video) {
1001           image_destroy(host->clients[i].incoming_video);
1002           host->clients[i].incoming_video = NULL;
1003         }
1004         if (host->clients[i].incoming_audio) {
1005           ringbuffer_destroy(host->clients[i].incoming_audio);
1006           host->clients[i].incoming_audio = NULL;
1007         }
1008         host->clients[i].active = false;
1009         mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1010         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate media buffers for client");
1011         return 0;
1012       }
1013 
1014       uint32_t client_id = host->clients[i].client_id;
1015       host->client_count++;
1016 
1017       mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1018 
1019       // Invoke callback
1020       if (host->callbacks.on_client_join) {
1021         host->callbacks.on_client_join(host, client_id, host->user_data);
1022       }
1023 
1024       return client_id;
1025     }
1026   }
1027 
1028   mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1029   return 0;
1030 }

uint32_t session_host_add_memory_participant
  (session_host_t * host)


#include <src/common/session/host.h>


Add a memory participant (host's own media).


Parameters


host Host handle (must not be NULL)


Returns


Assigned participant ID on success, 0 on failure


Registers a memory participant for the host's own webcam/audio.
    This allows the host to participate in the session without network loopback.
    Media is injected directly into the mixer via
    session_host_inject_frame().




Note


on_client_join callback is invoked on successful
  registration.



Only one memory participant per host is supported.




Definition at line 1032 of file host.c.

1033   if (!host || !host->initialized) {
1034     return 0;
1035   }
1036 
1037   mutex_lock(&host->clients_mutex);
1038 
1039   // Check if we have room
1040   if (host->client_count >= host->max_clients) {
1041     mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1042     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_SESSION_FULL, "Maximum clients reached");
1043     return 0;
1044   }
1045 
1046   // Check if memory participant already exists (only one allowed)
1047   for (int i = 0; i < host->max_clients; i++) {
1048     if (host->clients[i].active && host->clients[i].participant_type == PARTICIPANT_TYPE_MEMORY) {
1049       mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1050       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Memory participant already exists");
1051       return 0;
1052     }
1053   }
1054 
1055   // Find empty slot
1056   for (int i = 0; i < host->max_clients; i++) {
1057     if (!host->clients[i].active) {
1058       host->clients[i].participant_type = PARTICIPANT_TYPE_MEMORY;
1059       host->clients[i].client_id = host->next_client_id++;
1060       host->clients[i].socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE; // No socket for memory participant
1061       SAFE_STRNCPY(host->clients[i].ip_address, "memory", sizeof(host->clients[i].ip_address));
1062       host->clients[i].port = 0;
1063       host->clients[i].active = true;
1064       host->clients[i].video_active = true;  // Memory participants always have active video/audio
1065       host->clients[i].audio_active = true;
1066       host->clients[i].connected_at = (uint64_t)time(NULL);
1067       host->clients[i].transport = NULL;
1068 
1069       // Allocate media buffers (same as network clients)
1070       host->clients[i].incoming_video = image_new(480, 270);
1071       host->clients[i].incoming_audio = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(float), 960 * 10);
1072

1074         if (host->clients[i].incoming_video) {

1078         if (host->clients[i].incoming_audio) {
1079           ringbuffer_destroy(host->clients[i].incoming_audio);
1080           host->clients[i].incoming_audio = NULL;
1081         }
1082         host->clients[i].active = false;
1083         mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1084         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate media buffers for memory participant");
1085         return 0;
1086       }

1089       host->client_count++;
1090 
1091       mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1092 
1093       log_info("Added memory participant with ID %u", participant_id);
1094 
1095       // Invoke callback
1096       if (host->callbacks.on_client_join) {
1097         host->callbacks.on_client_join(host, participant_id, host->user_data);
1098       }
1099 
1100       return participant_id;
1101     }
1102   }
1103 
1104   mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1105   return 0;
1106 }

asciichat_error_t session_host_broadcast_frame
  (session_host_t * host, const char * frame)


#include <src/common/session/host.h>


Broadcast ASCII frame to all clients.


Parameters


host Host handle (must not be NULL)


frame ASCII frame data (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Sends an ASCII frame to all connected clients.


Definition at line 1294 of file host.c.


1294                                                                                         {
1295   if (!host || !host->initialized || !frame) {
1296     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_host_broadcast_frame: invalid parameter");
1297   }
1298 
1299   if (!host->running) {
1300     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "session_host_broadcast_frame: not running");
1301   }
1302 
1303   // Broadcast ASCII frame to all connected clients
1304   size_t frame_len = strlen(frame) + 1; // Include null terminator
1305   asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
1306 
1307   mutex_lock(&host->clients_mutex);
1308   for (int i = 0; i < host->max_clients; i++) {
1309     if (host->clients[i].active && host->clients[i].socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1310       asciichat_error_t send_result =
1311           packet_send(host->clients[i].socket, PACKET_TYPE_ASCII_FRAME, (const void *)frame, frame_len);
1312       if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1313         log_warn("Failed to send ASCII frame to client %u", host->clients[i].client_id);
1314         result = send_result; // Store error but continue broadcasting to other clients
1315       }
1316     }
1317   }
1318   mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1319 
1320   return result;
1321 }

bool session_host_client_has_transport
  (session_host_t * host, uint32_t client_id)


#include <src/common/session/host.h>


Check if a specific client has an active alternative transport.
    Convenient way to check if a client is using an alternative transport
    (WebRTC, WebSocket, etc.) instead of raw TCP socket.




Parameters


host Host handle (must not be NULL)


client_id Client ID to check


Returns


true if alternative transport is set, false
  otherwise


Definition at line 1510 of file host.c.


1510                                                                                  {
1511   if (!host || !host->initialized) {
1512     return false;
1513   }
1514 
1515   mutex_lock(&host->clients_mutex);
1516 
1517   // Find client with matching ID
1518   for (int i = 0; i < host->max_clients; i++) {
1519     if (host->clients[i].active && host->clients[i].client_id == client_id) {
1520       bool has_transport = host->clients[i].transport != NULL;
1521       mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1522       return has_transport;
1523     }
1524   }
1525 
1526   mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1527   return false;
1528 }

session_host_t * session_host_create (const
  session_host_config_t * config)


#include <src/common/session/host.h>


Create a new session host.


Parameters


Config Module Host configuration
  (must not be NULL)


Returns


Pointer to host handle, or NULL on failure


Creates a session host with the specified configuration. The host
    is not started until session_host_start() is called.




Note


Call session_host_destroy() to free resources when
  done.



On failure, sets asciichat_errno with error details.




Definition at line 173 of file host.c.


173                                                                          {
174   if (!config) {
175     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_host_create: NULL config");
176     return NULL;
177   }
178 
179   // Allocate host
180   session_host_t *host = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(session_host_t), session_host_t *);
181 
182   // Copy configuration
183   host->port = config->port > 0 ? config->port : OPT_PORT_INT_DEFAULT;
184   host->max_clients = config->max_clients > 0 ? config->max_clients : SESSION_HOST_DEFAULT_MAX_CLIENTS;
185   host->encryption_enabled = config->encryption_enabled;
186 
187   if (config->ipv4_address) {
188     SAFE_STRNCPY(host->ipv4_address, config->ipv4_address, sizeof(host->ipv4_address));
189   }
190 
191   if (config->ipv6_address) {
192     SAFE_STRNCPY(host->ipv6_address, config->ipv6_address, sizeof(host->ipv6_address));
193   }
194 
195   if (config->key_path) {
196     SAFE_STRNCPY(host->key_path, config->key_path, sizeof(host->key_path));
197   }
198 
199   if (config->password) {
200     SAFE_STRNCPY(host->password, config->password, sizeof(host->password));
201   }
202 
203   host->callbacks = config->callbacks;
204   host->user_data = config->user_data;
205 
206   // Initialize sockets to invalid
207   host->socket_v4 = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
208   host->socket_v6 = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
209   host->running = false;
210 
211   // Allocate client array
212   host->clients = SAFE_CALLOC((size_t)host->max_clients, sizeof(session_host_client_t), session_host_client_t *);
213 
214   host->client_count = 0;
215   host->next_client_id = 1;
216 
217   // Initialize mutex
218   if (mutex_init(&host->clients_mutex, "host_clients") != 0) {
219     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize clients mutex");
220     SAFE_FREE(host->clients);
221     SAFE_FREE(host);
222     return NULL;
223   }
224 
225   host->initialized = true;
226   return host;
227 }

void
  session_host_destroy (session_host_t * host)


#include <src/common/session/host.h>


Destroy session host and free resources.


Parameters


host Host to destroy (can be NULL)


Stops if running, disconnects all clients, and releases all
    resources. Safe to call with NULL.


Definition at line 229 of file host.c.


229                                                 {
230   if (!host) {
231     return;
232   }
233 
234   // Stop if running
235   if (host->running) {
236     session_host_stop(host);
237   }
238 
239   // Clean up audio resources
240   if (host->audio_ctx) {
241     session_audio_destroy(host->audio_ctx);
242     host->audio_ctx = NULL;
243   }
244   if (host->opus_decoder) {
245     opus_codec_destroy(host->opus_decoder);
246     host->opus_decoder = NULL;
247   }
248 
249   // Close sockets
250   if (host->socket_v4 != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
251     socket_close(host->socket_v4);
252     host->socket_v4 = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
253   }
254   if (host->socket_v6 != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
255     socket_close(host->socket_v6);
256     host->socket_v6 = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
257   }
258 
259   // Free client array and per-client resources
260   if (host->clients) {
261     for (int i = 0; i < host->max_clients; i++) {
262       if (host->clients[i].incoming_video) {
263         image_destroy(host->clients[i].incoming_video);
264         host->clients[i].incoming_video = NULL;
265       }
266       if (host->clients[i].incoming_audio) {
267         ringbuffer_destroy(host->clients[i].incoming_audio);
268         host->clients[i].incoming_audio = NULL;
269       }
270     }
271     SAFE_FREE(host->clients);
272   }
273 
274   // Destroy mutex
275   mutex_destroy(&host->clients_mutex);
276 
277   // Clear sensitive data
278   memset(host->password, 0, sizeof(host->password));
279 
280   host->initialized = false;
281   SAFE_FREE(host);
282 }

asciichat_error_t session_host_inject_audio
  (session_host_t * host, uint32_t participant_id, const float *
  samples, size_t count)


#include <src/common/session/host.h>


Inject audio samples from memory participant.


Parameters


host Host handle (must not be NULL)


participant_id Memory participant ID


samples Audio sample buffer (float format, must not be NULL)


count Number of samples


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Injects audio samples directly into the mixer from a memory
    participant. This bypasses network I/O and is used when the host
    participates in the session with their own audio.




Note


Sample data is copied, so caller retains ownership.


Definition at line 1153 of file host.c.


1154                                                           {
1155   if (!host || !host->initialized || !samples || count == 0) {
1156     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_host_inject_audio: invalid parameters");
1157   }
1158 
1159   mutex_lock(&host->clients_mutex);
1160 
1161   // Find memory participant
1162   for (int i = 0; i < host->max_clients; i++) {
1163     if (host->clients[i].active && host->clients[i].client_id == participant_id &&
1164         host->clients[i].participant_type == PARTICIPANT_TYPE_MEMORY) {
1165 
1166       if (!host->clients[i].incoming_audio) {
1167         mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1168         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Memory participant has no audio buffer");
1169       }
1170 
1171       // Write samples to ringbuffer (one at a time)
1172       size_t written = 0;
1173       for (size_t j = 0; j < count; j++) {
1174         if (ringbuffer_write(host->clients[i].incoming_audio, &samples[j])) {
1175           written++;
1176         } else {
1177           break; // Buffer full
1178         }
1179       }
1180 
1181       if (written < count) {
1182         log_warn_every(NS_PER_MS_INT, "Audio ringbuffer full, dropped %zu samples", count - written);
1183       }
1184 
1185       host->clients[i].audio_active = true;
1186 
1187       mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1188       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1189     }
1190   }
1191 
1192   mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1193   return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "Memory participant not found");
1194 }

asciichat_error_t session_host_inject_frame
  (session_host_t * host, uint32_t participant_id, const
  image_t * frame)


#include <src/common/session/host.h>


Inject a video frame from memory participant.


Parameters


host Host handle (must not be NULL)


participant_id Memory participant ID


frame Video frame image (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Injects a video frame directly into the mixer from a memory
    participant. This bypasses network I/O and is used when the host
    participates in the session with their own webcam.




Note


Frame data is copied, so caller retains ownership.


Definition at line 1108 of file host.c.


1108                                                                                                                  {
1109   if (!host || !host->initialized || !frame) {
1110     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_host_inject_frame: invalid parameters");
1111   }
1112 
1113   mutex_lock(&host->clients_mutex);
1114 
1115   // Find memory participant
1116   for (int i = 0; i < host->max_clients; i++) {
1117     if (host->clients[i].active && host->clients[i].client_id == participant_id &&
1118         host->clients[i].participant_type == PARTICIPANT_TYPE_MEMORY) {
1119 
1120       if (!host->clients[i].incoming_video) {
1121         mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1122         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Memory participant has no video buffer");
1123       }
1124 
1125       // Copy frame data into buffer
1126       image_t *dest = host->clients[i].incoming_video;
1127       if (dest->w != frame->w || dest->h != frame->h) {
1128         // Reallocate if size changed
1129         image_destroy(dest);
1130         dest = image_new(frame->w, frame->h);
1131         if (!dest) {
1132           host->clients[i].incoming_video = NULL;
1133           mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1134           return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to reallocate video buffer");
1135         }
1136         host->clients[i].incoming_video = dest;
1137       }
1138 
1139       // Copy pixel data
1140       memcpy(dest->pixels, frame->pixels, frame->w * frame->h * sizeof(rgb_pixel_t));
1141 
1142       host->clients[i].video_active = true;
1143 
1144       mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1145       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1146     }
1147   }
1148 
1149   mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1150   return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "Memory participant not found");
1151 }

  (session_host_t * host, uint32_t client_id)


#include <src/common/session/host.h>


Remove a client by ID.


Parameters


host Host handle (must not be NULL)


client_id Client ID to remove


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Disconnects and removes a client from the session.




Note


on_client_leave callback is invoked before removal.


Definition at line 1196 of file host.c.


1196                                                                                        {
1197   if (!host || !host->initialized) {
1198     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_host_remove_client: invalid host");
1199   }
1200 
1201   mutex_lock(&host->clients_mutex);
1202 
1203   for (int i = 0; i < host->max_clients; i++) {
1204     if (host->clients[i].active && host->clients[i].client_id == client_id) {
1205       // Invoke callback before removing
1206       mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1207       if (host->callbacks.on_client_leave) {
1208         host->callbacks.on_client_leave(host, client_id, host->user_data);
1209       }
1210       mutex_lock(&host->clients_mutex);
1211 
1212       // Close socket
1213       if (host->clients[i].socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1214         socket_close(host->clients[i].socket);
1215         host->clients[i].socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
1216       }
1217 
1218       // Clean up media buffers
1219       if (host->clients[i].incoming_video) {
1220         image_destroy(host->clients[i].incoming_video);
1221         host->clients[i].incoming_video = NULL;
1222       }
1223       if (host->clients[i].incoming_audio) {
1224         ringbuffer_destroy(host->clients[i].incoming_audio);
1225         host->clients[i].incoming_audio = NULL;
1226       }
1227 
1228       host->clients[i].active = false;
1229       host->client_count--;
1230 
1231       mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1232       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1233     }
1234   }
1235 
1236   mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1237   return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "Client not found: %u", client_id);
1238 }

asciichat_error_t session_host_set_client_transport
  (session_host_t * host, uint32_t client_id,
  acip_transport_t * transport)


#include <src/common/session/host.h>


Set an alternative transport for a specific client in the host.
    Allows replacing a client's default TCP socket transport with an alternative
    transport such as WebRTC DataChannel. Once set, the host will use this
    transport for send/receive operations with the specified client.




Parameters


host Host handle (must not be NULL)


client_id Client ID whose transport should be replaced


transport Alternative transport to use (can be NULL to clear)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, ERROR_NOT_FOUND if client not
  found, other error on failure


Note


This is used for WebRTC integration when DataChannel
  becomes ready



Transport ownership remains with caller (host does not free
  it)




If both socket and transport exist, transport takes precedence




Definition at line 1458 of file host.c.


1459                                                                                  {
1460   if (!host || !host->initialized) {
1461     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host is NULL or not initialized");
1462   }
1463 
1464   mutex_lock(&host->clients_mutex);
1465 
1466   // Find client with matching ID
1467   for (int i = 0; i < host->max_clients; i++) {
1468     if (host->clients[i].active && host->clients[i].client_id == client_id) {
1469       log_info("session_host_set_client_transport: Setting transport=%p for client %u (was=%p)", transport, client_id,
1470                host->clients[i].transport);
1471 
1472       host->clients[i].transport = transport;
1473 
1474       if (transport) {
1475         log_info("WebRTC transport now active for client %u", client_id);
1476       } else {
1477         log_info("WebRTC transport cleared for client %u, reverting to socket", client_id);
1478       }
1479 
1480       mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1481       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1482     }
1483   }
1484 
1485   mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
1486   log_warn("Client %u not found", client_id);
1487   return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "Client not found");
1488 }

Set display context for local frame rendering.


Parameters


host Host handle (must not be NULL)


display Display context for rendering frames locally (can be NULL to

Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Configures the host to render frames locally to the provided
    display context. This allows the host to see its own rendered output during
    testing. If display is NULL, local rendering is disabled.




Note


Can be called before or after render thread is
  started.

asciichat_error_t session_host_start
  (session_host_t * host)


#include <src/common/session/host.h>


Start accepting client connections.


Parameters


host Host handle (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Binds to the configured port and starts accepting connections.
    This function may start background threads for connection handling.


Definition at line 833 of file host.c.


833                                                            {
834   if (!host || !host->initialized) {
835     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_host_start: invalid host");
836   }
837 
838   if (host->running) {
839     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Already running
840   }
841 
842   // Create listen socket(s)
843   // For simplicity, we bind to IPv4 if specified, otherwise default to 0.0.0.0
844   const char *bind_address = host->ipv4_address[0] ? host->ipv4_address : "0.0.0.0";
845 
846   host->socket_v4 = create_listen_socket(bind_address, host->port);
847   if (host->socket_v4 == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
848     log_error("Failed to create IPv4 listen socket");
849     if (host->callbacks.on_error) {
850       host->callbacks.on_error(host, ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "Failed to create listen socket", host->user_data);
851     }
852     return GET_ERRNO();
853   }
854 
855   host->running = true;
856   log_info("Session host listening on %s:%d", bind_address, host->port);
857 
858   // Spawn accept loop thread
859   host->accept_thread_running = true;
860   if (asciichat_thread_create(&host->accept_thread, "accept", accept_loop_thread, host) != 0) {
861     log_error("Failed to spawn accept loop thread");
862     host->accept_thread_running = false;
863     if (host->callbacks.on_error) {
864       host->callbacks.on_error(host, ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to spawn accept loop thread", host->user_data);
865     }
866     socket_close(host->socket_v4);
867     host->socket_v4 = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
868     host->running = false;
869     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to spawn accept loop thread");
870   }
871 
872   // Spawn receive loop thread
873   host->receive_thread_running = true;
874   if (asciichat_thread_create(&host->receive_thread, "host_recv", receive_loop_thread, host) != 0) {
875     log_error("Failed to spawn receive loop thread");
876     host->receive_thread_running = false;
877     host->accept_thread_running = false;
878     asciichat_thread_join(&host->accept_thread, NULL);
879     if (host->callbacks.on_error) {
880       host->callbacks.on_error(host, ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to spawn receive loop thread", host->user_data);
881     }
882     socket_close(host->socket_v4);
883     host->socket_v4 = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
884     host->running = false;
885     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to spawn receive loop thread");
886   }
887 
888   // Spawn render thread (optional - can be started later)
889   // This thread handles video mixing and audio distribution
890   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
891 }

#include <src/common/session/host.h>


Start media rendering thread (video mixing and audio
    distribution).


Parameters


host Host handle (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Starts the render thread which collects video frames from all
    participants, generates mixed ASCII frames, and broadcasts them to all
    clients. Also handles audio mixing and distribution.




Note


The host must be running before starting the render
  thread.



Call session_host_stop_render() to stop the thread.

1371 
1372   if (!host->running) {
1373     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "session_host_start_render: not running");
1374   }
1375 
1376   if (host->render_thread_running) {
1377     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Already running
1378   }
1379 
1380   // Create audio context for mixing (host mode = true)
1381   if (!host->audio_ctx) {
1382     host->audio_ctx = session_audio_create(true);
1383     if (!host->audio_ctx) {
1384       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to create audio context");
1385     }
1386   }
1387 
1388   // Create Opus decoder for decoding incoming Opus audio (48kHz)
1389   if (!host->opus_decoder) {
1390     host->opus_decoder = opus_codec_create_decoder(48000);
1391     if (!host->opus_decoder) {
1392       session_audio_destroy(host->audio_ctx);
1393       host->audio_ctx = NULL;
1394       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to create Opus decoder");
1395     }
1396   }
1397 
1398   // Create Opus encoder for encoding mixed audio for broadcast (48kHz, VOIP mode, 24kbps)
1399   if (!host->opus_encoder) {
1400     host->opus_encoder = opus_codec_create_encoder(OPUS_APPLICATION_VOIP, 48000, 24000);
1401     if (!host->opus_encoder) {
1402       opus_codec_destroy(host->opus_decoder);
1403       host->opus_decoder = NULL;
1404       session_audio_destroy(host->audio_ctx);
1405       host->audio_ctx = NULL;
1406       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to create Opus encoder");
1407     }
1408   }
1409 
1410   // Spawn render thread
1411   host->render_thread_running = true;
1412   if (asciichat_thread_create(&host->render_thread, "render", host_render_thread, host) != 0) {
1413     log_error("Failed to spawn render thread");
1414     host->render_thread_running = false;
1415     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to spawn render thread");
1416   }
1417 
1418   log_info("Host render thread started");
1419   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1420 }

void
  session_host_stop (session_host_t * host)


#include <src/common/session/host.h>


Stop accepting connections and disconnect all clients.


Parameters


host Host handle (must not be NULL)


Gracefully stops the server, disconnects all clients, and cleans
    up.


Definition at line 893 of file host.c.


893                                              {
894   if (!host || !host->initialized || !host->running) {
895     return;
896   }
897 
898   // Stop render thread if running
899   if (host->render_thread_running) {
900     host->render_thread_running = false;
901     asciichat_thread_join(&host->render_thread, NULL);
902     log_info("Render thread joined");
903   }
904 
905   // Stop receive loop thread (reads from client sockets)
906   if (host->receive_thread_running) {
907     host->receive_thread_running = false;
908     asciichat_thread_join(&host->receive_thread, NULL);
909     log_info("Receive loop thread joined");
910   }
911 
912   // Stop accept loop thread (before closing listen socket)
913   if (host->accept_thread_running) {
914     host->accept_thread_running = false;
915     asciichat_thread_join(&host->accept_thread, NULL);
916     log_info("Accept loop thread joined");
917   }
918 
919   // Disconnect all clients
920   mutex_lock(&host->clients_mutex);
921   for (int i = 0; i < host->max_clients; i++) {
922     if (host->clients[i].active) {
923       // Invoke callback
924       if (host->callbacks.on_client_leave) {
925         host->callbacks.on_client_leave(host, host->clients[i].client_id, host->user_data);
926       }
927 
928       // Close socket
929       if (host->clients[i].socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
930         socket_close(host->clients[i].socket);
931         host->clients[i].socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
932       }
933 
934       host->clients[i].active = false;
935     }
936   }
937   host->client_count = 0;
938   mutex_unlock(&host->clients_mutex);
939 
940   // Close listen sockets
941   if (host->socket_v4 != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
942     socket_close(host->socket_v4);
943     host->socket_v4 = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
944   }
945   if (host->socket_v6 != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
946     socket_close(host->socket_v6);
947     host->socket_v6 = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
948   }
949 
950   host->running = false;
951 }

Create a new session capture context.

Config Module Capture
  configuration (must not be NULL)

Pointer to capture context, or NULL on failure


Creates and initializes a session capture context with the
    specified configuration. The media source is opened and ready for
  reading.




Note


Call session_capture_destroy() to free resources
  when done.



On failure, sets asciichat_errno with error details.

#include <src/common/session/capture.h>


Create network mode capture context without media source. Creates
    a minimal capture context for network modes (client, discovery) that receive
    video frames from the network instead of capturing locally. Only initializes
    keyboard and audio support; no media source is created.

153                                                                            {
154   // Network modes don't capture media - create minimal context for keyboard/audio only
155   session_capture_ctx_t *ctx = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(session_capture_ctx_t), session_capture_ctx_t *);
156   if (!ctx) {
157     return NULL;
158   }
159 
160   // Set FPS (use provided value or default to 60)
161   ctx->target_fps = target_fps > 0 ? target_fps : 60;
162   ctx->resize_for_network = false;
163 
164   // Audio and keyboard will be set up separately by callers
165   ctx->audio_enabled = false;
166   ctx->source = NULL;
167 
168   // Initialize adaptive sleep for consistency
169   uint64_t baseline_sleep_ns = NS_PER_SEC_INT / ctx->target_fps;
170   adaptive_sleep_config_t sleep_config = {.baseline_sleep_ns = baseline_sleep_ns,
171                                           .min_speed_multiplier = 0.5,
172                                           .max_speed_multiplier = 2.0,
173                                           .speedup_rate = 0.1,
174                                           .slowdown_rate = 0.1};
175   adaptive_sleep_init(&ctx->sleep_state, &sleep_config);
176 
177   // Initialize minimal FPS tracker (won't be used but keep structure consistent)
178   char *tracker_name = SAFE_MALLOC(32, char *);
179   if (!tracker_name) {
180     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
181     return NULL;
182   }
183   safe_snprintf(tracker_name, 32, "NETWORK_CAPTURE");
184   fps_init(&ctx->fps_tracker, 60, tracker_name);
185 
186   ctx->start_time_ns = time_get_ns();
187   ctx->initialized = true;
188 
189   log_debug("Created network capture context (no local media source)");
190   return ctx;
191 }

Connect to session server.


Parameters


p Participant handle (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Initiates connection to the configured server. Connection
    establishment is synchronous but callbacks are invoked asynchronously.




Note


on_connected callback is invoked on successful
  connection.



on_error callback is invoked on failure.




Definition at line 273 of file participant.c.


273                                                                         {
274   log_debug("session_participant_connect: enter - p=%p", p);
275 
276   if (!p || !p->initialized) {
277     log_debug("session_participant_connect: invalid param or not initialized");
278     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_participant_connect: invalid participant");
279   }
280 
281   log_debug("session_participant_connect: p->initialized=%d, p->connected=%d", p->initialized, p->connected);
282 
283   if (p->connected) {
284     log_debug("session_participant_connect: already connected, returning OK");
285     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Already connected
286   }
287 
288   // Create TCP connection to server
289   log_debug("session_participant_connect: calling connect_to_server(%s, %d)", p->address, p->port);
290   p->socket = connect_to_server(p->address, p->port);
291   log_debug("session_participant_connect: connect_to_server returned socket=%d (INVALID=%d)", p->socket,
292             INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE);
293 
294   if (p->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
295     log_error("Failed to connect to server at %s:%d", p->address, p->port);
296     if (p->callbacks.on_error) {
297       p->callbacks.on_error(p, ERROR_NETWORK_CONNECT, "Failed to connect to server", p->user_data);
298     }
299     return GET_ERRNO();
300   }
301 
302   log_debug("session_participant_connect: setting p->connected=true");
303   p->connected = true;
304   p->client_id = 0; // Will be assigned by server
305 
306   log_info("Connected to server at %s:%d (socket=%d)", p->address, p->port, p->socket);
307   log_debug("session_participant_connect: p->connected=%d after setting", p->connected);
308 
309   // Invoke callback
310   if (p->callbacks.on_connected) {
311     p->callbacks.on_connected(p, p->client_id, p->user_data);
312   }
313 
314   log_debug("session_participant_connect: returning ASCIICHAT_OK");
315   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
316 }

138   } else {

156   p->enable_video = config->enable_video;
157   p->callbacks = config->callbacks;
158   p->user_data = config->user_data;
159 
160   // Initialize socket to invalid
161   p->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
162   p->transport = NULL; // No alternative transport initially
163   p->connected = false;
164   p->client_id = 0;
165   p->video_active = false;
166   p->audio_active = false;
167 
168   // Initialize settings
169   session_settings_init(&p->settings);
170 
171   p->initialized = true;

173            p->connected);
174   return p;
175 }

215   // Clear sensitive data
216   memset(p->password, 0, sizeof(p->password));
217   memset(p->server_key, 0, sizeof(p->server_key));
218 
219   p->initialized = false;
220   SAFE_FREE(p);
221 }

void
  session_participant_disconnect (session_participant_t * p)


#include
  <src/common/session/participant.h>


Disconnect from session server.


Parameters


p Participant handle (must not be NULL)


Gracefully disconnects from the server and stops all streams.




Note


on_disconnected callback is invoked after
  disconnection.


Definition at line 318 of file participant.c.


318                                                               {
319   if (!p || !p->initialized) {
320     return;
321   }
322 
323   if (!p->connected) {
324     return;
325   }
326 
327   // Stop media streams
328   if (p->video_active) {
329     session_participant_stop_video(p);
330   }
331   if (p->audio_active) {
332     session_participant_stop_audio(p);
333   }
334 
335   // Close socket
336   if (p->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
337     socket_close(p->socket);
338     p->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
339   }
340 
341   p->connected = false;
342   p->client_id = 0;
343 
344   // Invoke callback
345   if (p->callbacks.on_disconnected) {
346     p->callbacks.on_disconnected(p, p->user_data);
347   }
348 }

#include
  <src/common/session/participant.h>


Set an alternative transport for the participant. Allows replacing

    send/receive operations.

p Participant handle (must not be NULL)


transport Alternative transport to use (can be NULL to clear)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Note


This is used for WebRTC integration when DataChannel
  becomes ready



Transport ownership remains with caller (participant does not free
    it)




If both socket and transport exist, transport takes precedence

735

737

asciichat_error_t session_participant_start_audio_capture
  (session_participant_t * p)


#include
  <src/common/session/participant.h>


Start audio capture and transmission to host.


Parameters


p Participant handle (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Spawns a background thread that:


    
  
  • Captures audio from microphone
    • 
  
  • Encodes to Opus (lossy compression)
    • 
  
  • Sends AUDIO packets to the host
    • 



Audio capture respects the participant's enable_audio flag and
    connection state. Multiple calls are safe (returns OK if already
  running).




Note


Requires connection to be established first via
  session_participant_connect()



Use session_participant_stop_audio_capture() to stop
    transmission




Definition at line 650 of file participant.c.


650                                                                                     {
651   if (!p || !p->initialized) {
652     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_participant_start_audio_capture: invalid participant");
653   }
654 
655   if (!p->connected) {
656     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "session_participant_start_audio_capture: not connected");
657   }
658 
659   if (!p->enable_audio) {
660     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "session_participant_start_audio_capture: audio not enabled");
661   }
662 
663   if (p->audio_capture_running) {
664     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Already running
665   }
666 
667   // Create audio capture context if not already created
668   if (!p->audio_capture) {
669     p->audio_capture = session_audio_create(false); // false = participant mode (no mixing)
670     if (!p->audio_capture) {
671       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to create audio capture context");
672     }
673   }
674 
675   // Start audio capture and playback
676   asciichat_error_t err = session_audio_start_duplex(p->audio_capture);
677   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
678     return SET_ERRNO(err, "Failed to start audio duplex");
679   }
680 
681   // Create Opus encoder for audio compression (48kHz, VOIP mode, 24 kbps)
682   if (!p->opus_encoder) {
683     p->opus_encoder = opus_codec_create_encoder(OPUS_APPLICATION_VOIP, 48000, 24000);
684     if (!p->opus_encoder) {
685       session_audio_stop(p->audio_capture);
686       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to create Opus encoder");
687     }
688   }
689 
690   // Spawn audio capture thread
691   p->audio_capture_running = true;
692   if (asciichat_thread_create(&p->audio_capture_thread, "audio_capture", participant_audio_capture_thread, p) != 0) {
693     log_error("Failed to spawn audio capture thread");
694     p->audio_capture_running = false;
695     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to spawn audio capture thread");
696   }
697 
698   log_info("Audio capture started");
699   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
700 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, asciichat_thread_create(),
    session_participant::audio_capture,
    session_participant::audio_capture_running,
    session_participant::audio_capture_thread,
    session_participant::connected,
    session_participant::enable_audio, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_THREAD,
    session_participant::initialized, log_error, log_info,
    OPUS_APPLICATION_VOIP, opus_codec_create_encoder(),
    session_participant::opus_encoder, session_audio_create(),
    session_audio_start_duplex(), session_audio_stop(), and
    SET_ERRNO.

asciichat_error_t session_participant_start_video_capture
  (session_participant_t * p)


#include
  <src/common/session/participant.h>


Start video capture and transmission to host.


Parameters


p Participant handle (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Spawns a background thread that:


    
  
  • Captures video from webcam/file/test pattern
    • 
  
  • Resizes for network efficiency (bandwidth optimization)
    • 
  
  • Sends IMAGE_FRAME packets to the host
    • 



Video capture respects the participant's enable_video flag and
    connection state. Multiple calls are safe (returns OK if already
  running).




Note


Requires connection to be established first via
  session_participant_connect()



Use session_participant_stop_video_capture() to stop
    transmission




Definition at line 589 of file participant.c.


589                                                                                     {
590   if (!p || !p->initialized) {
591     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_participant_start_video_capture: invalid participant");
592   }
593 
594   if (!p->connected) {
595     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "session_participant_start_video_capture: not connected");
596   }
597 
598   if (!p->enable_video) {
599     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "session_participant_start_video_capture: video not enabled");
600   }
601 
602   if (p->video_capture_running) {
603     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Already running
604   }
605 
606   // Create video capture context if not already created
607   if (!p->video_capture) {
608     session_capture_config_t config = {
609         .type = MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM,
610         .path = "0", // Default device
611         .target_fps = 60,
612         .resize_for_network = true, // Optimize for bandwidth
613     };
614     p->video_capture = session_capture_create(&config);
615     if (!p->video_capture) {
616       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to create video capture context");
617     }
618   }
619 
620   // Spawn video capture thread (media source is ready in ctx)
621   p->video_capture_running = true;
622   if (asciichat_thread_create(&p->video_capture_thread, "video_capture", participant_video_capture_thread, p) != 0) {
623     log_error("Failed to spawn video capture thread");
624     p->video_capture_running = false;
625     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to spawn video capture thread");
626   }
627 
628   log_info("Video capture started");
629   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
630 }

asciichat_error_t session_render_loop
  (session_capture_ctx_t * capture, session_display_ctx_t *
  display, session_should_exit_fn should_exit, session_capture_fn
  capture_cb, session_sleep_for_frame_fn sleep_cb,
  session_keyboard_handler_fn keyboard_handler, void * user_data)


#include <src/common/session/render.h>


Unified render loop for all display modes. Flexible render loop
    that supports both synchronous and event-driven modes. Optionally integrates
    keyboard input handling for interactive controls.




Synchronous Mode (capture != NULL, callbacks == NULL):
    Handles complete render lifecycle:


    
  
  • Frame rate limiting via session_capture_sleep_for_fps()
    • 
  
  • Frame capture via session_capture_read_frame()
    • 
  
  • ASCII conversion via session_display_convert_to_ascii()
    • 
  
  • Terminal rendering via session_display_render_frame()
    • 
  
  • Keyboard input polling and handling (if handler provided)
    • 
  
  • Memory cleanup with SAFE_FREE()
    • 



Used by: mirror mode, discovery participant mode




Event-Driven Mode (capture == NULL, callbacks != NULL):
    Delegates all frame operations to callbacks:


    
  
  • Custom frame capture via capture callback (can return NULL)
    • 
  
  • Custom timing via sleep_for_frame callback
    • 
  
  • ASCII conversion via session_display_convert_to_ascii()
    • 
  
  • Terminal rendering via session_display_render_frame()
    • 
  
  • Keyboard input polling and handling (if handler provided)
    • 
  
  • Memory cleanup with SAFE_FREE()
    • 



Used by: client mode, async frame sources




Keyboard Support:


    
  
  • If keyboard_handler is provided, initializes keyboard input at loop
    start
    • 
  
  • Polls for keyboard input each frame iteration
    • 
  
  • Calls keyboard_handler callback when keys are pressed
    • 
  
  • Cleans up keyboard on loop exit
    • 
  
  • Safe to pass NULL for no keyboard support (backward compatible)
    • 



Parameters


capture Capture context for synchronous mode (NULL
  if using callbacks)


display Display context (must not be NULL)


should_exit Callback to check exit condition each
  iteration (must not be NULL)


capture_cb Custom capture callback for event-driven mode (NULL if using
  capture context)


sleep_cb Custom sleep callback for event-driven mode (NULL if using
  capture context)


keyboard_handler Optional keyboard handler callback (NULL for no keyboard
  support)


user_data Opaque pointer passed to all callbacks


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Note


Either capture XOR (capture_cb && sleep_cb) must
  be provided, not both



Caller must free capture and display contexts after this
  returns




In synchronous mode, capture callback can return NULL on frame
    unavailability




In event-driven mode, capture callback can return NULL
  gracefully




keyboard_handler is optional - pass NULL to disable keyboard
    support




Synchronous Example (Mirror Mode)








static bool my_should_exit(void *user_data) {


  return atomic_load(&g_exit_flag);
}
int main(void) {


  session_capture_ctx_t *capture = session_capture_create(NULL);


  session_display_ctx_t *display = session_display_create(NULL);


  platform_set_console_ctrl_handler(my_ctrl_handler);


  asciichat_error_t result = session_render_loop(


      capture, display, my_should_exit,


      NULL, NULL,  // No custom callbacks


      NULL         // No user_data needed


  );


  session_display_destroy(display);


  session_capture_destroy(capture);


  return result == ASCIICHAT_OK ? 0 : (int)result;
}





Event-Driven Example (Client Mode)








static image_t *client_capture_frame(void *user_data) {


  client_ctx_t *ctx = (client_ctx_t *)user_data;


  return network_frame_queue_pop_nonblocking(ctx->frame_queue);
}
static void client_sleep_for_frame(void *user_data) {


  client_ctx_t *ctx = (client_ctx_t *)user_data;


  if (network_frame_queue_empty(ctx->frame_queue)) {


    platform_sleep_ms(10);


  }
}
static bool client_should_exit(void *user_data) {


  return !server_connection_is_active();
}
int main(void) {


  session_display_ctx_t *display = session_display_create(NULL);


  client_ctx_t client_ctx = {...};


  asciichat_error_t result = session_render_loop(


      NULL, display, client_should_exit,  // NULL capture = event-driven


      client_capture_frame,               // Custom capture


      client_sleep_for_frame,             // Custom sleep


      &client_ctx                         // Context for callbacks


  );


  session_display_destroy(display);


  return result == ASCIICHAT_OK ? 0 : (int)result;
}

    common/session/render.c.

59   // Validate required parameters
