61            (void *)capture, (void *)display, capture ? "SYNCHRONOUS" : "EVENT-DRIVEN");
62 
63   if (!display) {
64     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_render_loop: display context is NULL");
65     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
66   }
67 
68   if (!should_exit) {
69     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_render_loop: should_exit callback is NULL");
70     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
71   }
72 
73   // Validate mode: either capture context OR custom callbacks, not both
74   if (!capture && !capture_cb) {
75     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_render_loop: must provide either capture context or capture callback");
76     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
77   }
78 
79   if (capture && capture_cb) {
80     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_render_loop: cannot provide both capture context and capture callback");
81     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
82   }
83 
84   // In event-driven mode, both capture_cb and sleep_cb must be provided together
85   if (capture_cb && !sleep_cb) {
86     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_render_loop: capture_cb requires sleep_cb (must provide both or neither)");
87     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
88   }
89 
90   if (sleep_cb && !capture_cb) {
91     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_render_loop: sleep_cb requires capture_cb (must provide both or neither)");
92     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
93   }
94 
95   // SYNCHRONOUS MODE: Use threaded pipeline for capture + render
96   if (capture != NULL) {
97     log_info("[SESSION_RENDER_LOOP_SYNCHRONOUS] Creating pipeline... (capture=%p, display=%p)", (void*)capture, (void*)display);
98     session_pipeline_t *pipeline = NULL;
99     asciichat_error_t pipeline_err = session_pipeline_create(capture, display, &pipeline);
100     log_info("[SESSION_RENDER_LOOP_SYNCHRONOUS] pipeline_create returned: %d (pipeline=%p)", pipeline_err, (void*)pipeline);
101     if (pipeline_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
102       log_error("[SESSION_RENDER_LOOP_SYNCHRONOUS] Pipeline creation failed: %d", pipeline_err);
103       return pipeline_err;
104     }
105     log_info("[SESSION_RENDER_LOOP_SYNCHRONOUS] Pipeline created successfully, running main loop...");
106 
107     asciichat_error_t run_err = session_pipeline_run_main(pipeline, should_exit, keyboard_handler, user_data);
108 
109     // In snapshot mode, pass actual capture-based elapsed time to encoder for correct video duration
110     if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && display && g_snapshot_first_capture_ns > 0) {
111       uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
112       uint64_t elapsed_ns = now_ns - g_snapshot_first_capture_ns;
113       double capture_elapsed_sec = (double)elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
114       log_info("SNAPSHOT: Pipeline finished - passing capture_elapsed=%.3f to encoder", capture_elapsed_sec);
115       session_display_set_snapshot_actual_duration(display, capture_elapsed_sec);
116     }
117 
118     session_pipeline_destroy(pipeline);
119     return run_err;
120   } else {
121     log_warn("[SESSION_RENDER_LOOP] capture is NULL - will not use pipeline (capture_cb=%p)", (void*)capture_cb);
122   }
123 
124   // Snapshot mode state tracking
125   bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
126   bool snapshot_done = false;
127   uint64_t frames_rendered_since_first = 0; // Track frames rendered after first frame (for snapshot delay)
128   // NOTE: g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered is set by display.c when first frame is rendered via platform_write_all()
129 
130   // Help screen state tracking for clear-screen transition
131   bool help_was_active = false;
132 
133   // Terminal resize tracking (for auto_width/auto_height mode)
134   unsigned short int last_terminal_width = terminal_get_effective_width();
135   unsigned short int last_terminal_height = terminal_get_effective_height();
136 
137   log_info("session_render_loop: STARTING - display=%p capture=%p capture_cb=%p snapshot_mode=%s snapshot_delay=%.2f",
138            (void *)display, (void *)capture, (void *)capture_cb, snapshot_mode ? "YES" : "NO",
139            snapshot_mode ? GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay) : 0.0);
140   log_info("session_render_loop: About to enter main loop, should_exit fn check...");
141   if (should_exit(user_data)) {
142     log_warn("session_render_loop: EXIT signal already set at entry, aborting render loop");
143     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
144   }
145   log_info("session_render_loop: OK to proceed with main loop");
146 
147 
148   // Keyboard input initialization (if keyboard handler is provided)
149   // Disable keyboard only in snapshot mode
150   // Keyboard is pre-initialized from asciichat_shared_init()
151   // Allow keyboard input in all other modes if handler provided
152   bool keyboard_enabled = keyboard_handler && !snapshot_mode;
153 
154   // Pause mode state tracking (for pause/unpause transitions in event-driven mode)
155   bool initial_paused_frame_rendered = false;
156   bool is_paused = false;
157   log_info("render_loop: Keyboard setup - handler=%p snapshot=%s enabled=%s", (void *)keyboard_handler,
158            snapshot_mode ? "YES" : "NO", keyboard_enabled ? "YES" : "NO");
159 
160   // Log TTY detection status for debugging keyboard issues
161   log_info("TTY Status: stdin_tty=%d stdout_tty=%d interactive=%d keyboard_enabled=%d", terminal_is_stdin_tty(),
162            terminal_is_stdout_tty(), terminal_is_interactive(), keyboard_enabled);
163 
164   // Frame rate timing (event-driven mode only)
165   uint64_t frame_count = 0;
166   uint64_t frame_start_ns = 0;
167 
168   // Event-driven mode: disable console logging during rendering to prevent logs from corrupting frame display
169   // This must be done BEFORE the render loop to avoid repeated lock/unlock cycles that could cause deadlocks
170   log_info("[EVENT_DRIVEN_MODE] Entering render loop. snapshot_mode=%s", snapshot_mode ? "YES" : "NO");
171   log_set_terminal_output(false);
172 
173   // Main render loop
174   log_debug("session_render_loop: entering main loop");
175   int loop_iteration = 0;
176   log_info("[RENDER_LOOP_START] snapshot_mode=%s, snapshot_delay=%.2f", snapshot_mode ? "YES" : "NO",
177            snapshot_mode ? GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay) : 0.0);
178 
179   // Wait for splash animation to complete before rendering first frame
180   // This prevents ASCII art from mixing with splash screen output
181   // But skip for immediate snapshots (snapshot-delay=0) which need to output first frame immediately
182   static bool splash_wait_done = false;
183   if (!splash_wait_done) {
184     splash_wait_done = true;
185     if (!(GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay) == 0.0)) {
186       // Re-enable logging briefly for splash wait, then disable again
187       log_set_terminal_output(true);
188       splash_wait_for_animation();
189       log_set_terminal_output(false);
190     }
191   }
192 
193   // In snapshot mode, we must render at least one frame before checking should_exit
194   // to ensure the snapshot delay timer can start
195   bool force_first_iteration = snapshot_mode && frame_count == 0;
196 
197   // Register display context globally so signal handlers (Ctrl+C) can access it
198   // This enables Ctrl+C to close the help screen instead of quitting when help is active
199   session_display_set_global_context(display);
200 
201   while (force_first_iteration || (!should_exit(user_data) && !(snapshot_mode && snapshot_done))) {
202     force_first_iteration = false;
203     loop_iteration++;
204     log_info("[LOOP_ITER] iteration=%d, snapshot_done=%s, frame_count=%lu", loop_iteration, snapshot_done ? "YES" : "NO", frame_count);
205     if (loop_iteration % 60 == 0) {
206       log_debug("session_render_loop: iteration %d, should_exit check returning false", loop_iteration);
207     }
208     // Snapshot mode: exit at start of iteration if done
209     // This prevents frame 2+ from being captured when snapshot_delay has elapsed
210     if (snapshot_mode && snapshot_done && frame_count > 0) {
211       log_info("[SNAPSHOT_EXIT] Snapshot mode: exiting at loop iteration start (snapshot_done=true, frames=%lu)", frame_count);
212       break;
213     }
214 
215     log_debug_every(US_PER_SEC_INT, "session_render_loop: frame %lu", frame_count);
216     // Frame timing - measure total time to maintain target FPS
217     frame_start_ns = time_get_ns();
218 
219     // Frame capture and timing - mode-dependent
220     image_t *image = NULL;
221     uint64_t capture_start_ns = 0;
222     uint64_t capture_end_ns = 0;
223     uint64_t pre_convert_ns = 0;
224     uint64_t post_convert_ns = 0;
225     uint64_t conversion_elapsed_ns = 0;
226 
227     // EVENT-DRIVEN MODE: Frames come from callbacks
228     // Both sleep_cb and capture_cb are guaranteed non-NULL by validation above
229     sleep_cb(user_data);
230     image = capture_cb(user_data);
231 
232     if (!image) {
233       // No frame available - this is normal in async modes (network latency, etc.)
234       // Just continue to next iteration, don't exit
235       continue;
236     }
237 
238     // Event-driven mode: increment frame count
239     frame_count++;
240 
241     // Check for terminal resize (if auto_width or auto_height is enabled)
242     // This allows the render to adapt immediately when the user resizes the terminal
243     bool auto_width = GET_OPTION(auto_width);
244     bool auto_height = GET_OPTION(auto_height);
245     if (auto_width || auto_height) {
246       unsigned short int current_width = 0;
247       unsigned short int current_height = 0;
248 
249       asciichat_error_t size_err = get_terminal_size(&current_width, &current_height);
250       if (size_err == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
251         bool width_changed = auto_width && (current_width != last_terminal_width);
252         bool height_changed = auto_height && (current_height != last_terminal_height);
253 
254         if (width_changed || height_changed) {
255           if (width_changed) {
256             options_set_int("width", current_width);
257             log_info("Terminal width changed: %u → %u", last_terminal_width, current_width);
258             last_terminal_width = current_width;
259           }
260           if (height_changed) {
261             options_set_int("height", current_height);
262             log_info("Terminal height changed: %u → %u", last_terminal_height, current_height);
263             last_terminal_height = current_height;
264           }
265           // Clear screen when terminal is resized to avoid visual artifacts
266           terminal_clear_screen();
267         }
268       }
269     }
270 
271     // Convert image to ASCII using display context
272     // Handles all palette, terminal caps, width, height, stretch settings
273     pre_convert_ns = time_get_ns();
274     char *ascii_frame = session_display_convert_to_ascii(display, image);
275     post_convert_ns = time_get_ns();
276     conversion_elapsed_ns = post_convert_ns - pre_convert_ns;
277 
278 
279     if (frame_count <= 3) {
280       log_info("RENDER_LOOP[%lu]: image=%p, ascii_frame=%p (conversion took %u ms)",
281                frame_count, (void *)image, (void *)ascii_frame,
282                (unsigned)(conversion_elapsed_ns / NS_PER_MS_INT));
283     }
284 
285     // Declare variables that need scope beyond the if (ascii_frame) block
286     bool is_paused_frame = initial_paused_frame_rendered && is_paused;
287     bool output_paused_frame = snapshot_mode && is_paused_frame;
288     uint64_t pre_render_ns = 0, post_render_ns = 0;
289 
290     if (ascii_frame) {
291       log_info_every(1 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, "render_loop: ascii_frame ready (len=%zu)", strlen(ascii_frame));
292 
293       // Always attempt to render frames; the display context will handle filtering based on:
294       // - TTY mode: render all frames with cursor control (even in snapshot mode, for animation)
295       // - Piped mode: render all frames without cursor control (for continuous capture)
296       // - Snapshot mode on non-TTY: only the display context renders the final frame
297       // BUT: don't render if splash screen is still animating - wait for it to finish
298       bool splash_running = splash_is_running();
299       bool should_write = !splash_running;
300       if (frame_count == 1) {
301         log_dev("[RENDER_FRAME] Frame 1: splash_is_running=%s, should_write=%s", splash_running ? "YES" : "NO", should_write ? "YES" : "NO");
302       }
303       if (should_write) {
304         // is_final = true when: snapshot done, or paused frame (for both snapshot and pause modes)
305         // Profile: render frame
306         pre_render_ns = time_get_ns();
307         START_TIMER("render_frame");
308 
309         if (frame_count <= 5 || frame_count % 10 == 0) {
310           log_info("RENDER_LOOP: Frame %lu - calling session_display_render_frame, display=%p",
311                    (unsigned long)frame_count, (void *)display);
312         }
313 
314         // Check if SIGINT handler cancelled help mode (Ctrl+C while help is open).
315         // The signal handler sets an atomic flag instead of touching state directly.
316         if (display && keyboard_help_check_signal_cancel()) {
317           keyboard_help_toggle(display);
318           log_debug("Ctrl+C cancelled help screen - returning to ASCII art");
319         }
320 
321         // Check if help screen is active - if so, render help instead of frame
322         // Help screen is disabled in snapshot mode and non-interactive terminals (keyboard disabled)
323         bool help_is_active = display && keyboard_help_is_active(display);
324 
325         // Detect transition from help to ASCII art rendering
326         // When help closes, clear the screen before rendering ASCII art
327         if (help_was_active && !help_is_active) {
328           terminal_clear_screen();
329           log_debug_every(1 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, "Cleared screen when transitioning from help to ASCII art");
330         }
331 
332         if (help_is_active) {
333           keyboard_help_render(display);
334         } else {
335           session_display_render_frame(display, ascii_frame);
336         }
337 
338         // Snapshot mode: increment frame counter for all displayed frames
339         // Counter starts at 1 for the first displayed frame, then 2, 3, 4... for subsequent frames
340         // Timer is started by display.c when g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered is set
341         if (snapshot_mode && g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered) {
342           frames_rendered_since_first++;
343           log_info_every(1 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, "[SNAPSHOT] Frame rendered: frames_rendered_since_first=%lu", frames_rendered_since_first);
344         }
345 
346         // Update help state for next iteration
347         help_was_active = help_is_active;
348 
349         STOP_TIMER("render_frame");
350       }
351 
352       // Keyboard input polling (if enabled) - MUST come before snapshot exit check so help screen can be toggled
353       // Allow keyboard in snapshot mode too (for help screen toggle debugging)
354       // Only enable keyboard if BOTH stdin AND stdout are TTYs to avoid buffering issues
355       // when tcsetattr() modifies the tty line discipline
356       if (keyboard_enabled && keyboard_handler) {
357         START_TIMER("keyboard_read_%lu", (unsigned long)frame_count);
358         keyboard_key_t key = keyboard_read_nonblocking();
359         double keyboard_elapsed_ns = STOP_TIMER("keyboard_read_%lu", (unsigned long)frame_count);
360         if (keyboard_elapsed_ns >= 0.0) {
361           char _duration_str[32];
362           time_pretty((uint64_t)keyboard_elapsed_ns, -1, _duration_str, sizeof(_duration_str));
363           log_dev_every(1 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, "RENDER[%lu] Keyboard read complete (key=%d) in %s",
364                         (unsigned long)frame_count, key, _duration_str);
365         }
366         if (key != KEY_NONE) {
367           log_debug("KEYBOARD: Key pressed: code=%d char='%c'", key, (key >= 32 && key < 127) ? key : '?');
368           // Check if interactive grep should handle this key
369           if (log_search_should_handle(key)) {
370             log_debug("KEYBOARD: Grep handler taking key %d", key);
371             log_search_handle_key(key);
372             continue; // Force immediate re-render
373           }
374 
375           // Normal keyboard handler
376           log_debug("KEYBOARD: Normal handler taking key %d", key);
377           keyboard_handler(capture, key, user_data);
378         }
379       }
380 
381 
382       // Free frame before checking exit conditions to avoid double-free
383       SAFE_FREE(ascii_frame);
384     }
385 
386     // Snapshot mode: check if elapsed time has reached snapshot_delay duration (terminal render timing)
387     // Separate from capture timing - both should measure their own snapshot_delay
388     if (snapshot_mode && !snapshot_done) {
389       if (g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered && g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns > 0) {
390         double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
391         uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
392         uint64_t elapsed_ns = now_ns - g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns;
393         double elapsed_sec = (double)elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
394 
395         if (frames_rendered_since_first == 1 || frames_rendered_since_first % 20 == 0) {
396           log_info("SNAPSHOT: RENDER_CHECK iter=%lu elapsed=%.3f target=%.2f (from first_frame_rendered)",
397                    frames_rendered_since_first, elapsed_sec, snapshot_delay);
398         }
399 
400         // snapshot_delay=0 means exit after first frame
401         // snapshot_delay>0 means wait that many seconds before exiting
402         // Exit is based on elapsed wall-clock time, not frame count
403         bool should_exit = (snapshot_delay == 0.0) || (elapsed_sec >= snapshot_delay);
404 
405         if (should_exit) {
406           log_info("SNAPSHOT: RENDER_DONE at iteration %lu - elapsed=%.3f target=%.2f (setting snapshot_done=true)",
407                    frames_rendered_since_first, elapsed_sec, snapshot_delay);
408           // We don't end frames with newlines so the next log would print on the same line as the frame's
409           // last row without an \n here. We only need this \n in stdout when interactive,
410           // so piped snapshots don't have a weird newline in stdout that they don't need.
411           if (terminal_is_interactive()) {
412             printf("\n");
413           }
414           snapshot_done = true;
415         }
416       } else {
417         if (frames_rendered_since_first == 0) {
418           log_debug("SNAPSHOT: Waiting for first_frame_rendered (g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered=%d, g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns=%llu)",
419                     g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered, (unsigned long long)g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns);
420         }
421       }
422     }
423 
424     // Exit conditions: snapshot mode exits after capturing the final frame or initial paused frame
425     if (snapshot_mode && (snapshot_done || output_paused_frame)) {
426       log_info("SNAPSHOT: EXIT CONDITION MET - snapshot_done=%d, output_paused_frame=%d", snapshot_done, output_paused_frame);
427 
428       // Calculate elapsed time from first frame captured (for video file)
429       // This is separate from render time - video should span from first capture to last capture
430       double capture_elapsed_sec = 0.0;
431       if (g_snapshot_first_capture_ns > 0) {
432         uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
433         uint64_t elapsed_ns = now_ns - g_snapshot_first_capture_ns;
434         capture_elapsed_sec = (double)elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
435         log_info("SNAPSHOT: EXIT - Capture elapsed: %.3f seconds, render elapsed: %.3f seconds, g_snapshot_first_capture_ns=%llu",
436                  capture_elapsed_sec,
437                  (g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns > 0) ? (double)(now_ns - g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns) / NS_PER_SEC_INT : 0.0,
438                  (unsigned long long)g_snapshot_first_capture_ns);
439       } else {
440         log_warn("SNAPSHOT: EXIT condition met but g_snapshot_first_capture_ns is 0!");
441       }
442 
443       // Pass actual capture elapsed time to encoder for correct video duration
444       log_info("SNAPSHOT: About to call session_display_set_snapshot_actual_duration with duration=%.3f, display=%p",
445                capture_elapsed_sec, (void *)display);
446       if (display) {
447         session_display_set_snapshot_actual_duration(display, capture_elapsed_sec);
448       } else {
449         log_warn("SNAPSHOT: display context is NULL!");
450       }
451 
452       // Signal application to exit in snapshot mode
453       APP_CALLBACK_VOID(signal_exit);
454       break;
455     }
456 
457     // Frame rendering and timing details (moved down since snapshot exit is now above it)
458     if (ascii_frame) {
459 
460       // Measure frame completion right after rendering, BEFORE keyboard polling
461       // This gives us accurate timing for just the core frame operations
462       uint64_t frame_end_render_ns = time_get_ns();
463 
464       // Calculate each phase duration
465       uint64_t prestart_ms =
466           (capture_start_ns > frame_start_ns) ? (capture_start_ns - frame_start_ns) / NS_PER_MS_INT : 0;
467       uint64_t capture_ms =
468           (capture_end_ns > capture_start_ns) ? (capture_end_ns - capture_start_ns) / NS_PER_MS_INT : 0;
469       uint64_t convert_ms = conversion_elapsed_ns / NS_PER_MS_INT;
470       uint64_t render_ms =
471           (post_render_ns > pre_render_ns && post_render_ns > 0) ? (post_render_ns - pre_render_ns) / NS_PER_MS_INT : 0;
472       uint64_t total_ms =
473           (frame_end_render_ns > frame_start_ns) ? (frame_end_render_ns - frame_start_ns) / NS_PER_MS_INT : 0;
474 
475       // Log phase breakdown every 5 frames
476       if (frame_count % 5 == 0) {
477         log_dev_every(
478             2 * NS_PER_SEC_INT,
479             "PHASE_BREAKDOWN[%lu]: prestart=%llu ms, capture=%llu ms, convert=%llu ms, render=%llu ms (total=%llu ms)",
480             frame_count, prestart_ms, capture_ms, convert_ms, render_ms, total_ms);
481       }
482     } else {
483       // Snapshot mode: even if frame conversion failed, check if we should exit
484       // This ensures snapshot_delay is honored even if display context isn't rendering
485       if (snapshot_mode && snapshot_done) {
486         break;
487       }
488     }
489 
490     // In event-driven mode, frame rate limiting is handled by the sleep_cb callback
491     // Synchronous mode (with capture context) uses the pipeline and returns early above
492 
493     // Note: Images returned by media sources are cached/reused and should NOT be destroyed
494     // The image pointers are managed by the source and will be cleaned up on source shutdown
495   } // while (!should_exit(user_data)) {
496 
497   // Re-enable console logging after rendering completes
498   log_set_terminal_output(true);
499   if (!snapshot_mode && terminal_is_interactive()) {
500     printf("\n");
501   }
502 
503   // Keyboard input cleanup (if it was initialized)
504   if (keyboard_enabled) {
505     keyboard_destroy();
506     log_debug_every(2 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, "Keyboard input disabled");
507   }
508 
509   // Unregister global display context (for signal handlers)
510   session_display_set_global_context(NULL);
511 
512   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
513 }






References APP_CALLBACK_VOID, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    auto_height, auto_width, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    g_snapshot_first_capture_ns, g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered,
    g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns, GET_OPTION,
    get_terminal_size(), keyboard_destroy(),
    keyboard_help_check_signal_cancel(),

asciichat_error_t session_server_like_run (const
  session_server_like_config_t * config)


#include <src/common/session/server_like.h>Run a
    server-like mode with unified initialization and shutdown




This function orchestrates the complete lifecycle of server-like
    modes by handling common boilerplate (keepawake, signal handlers) while
    delegating mode-specific logic to callbacks. Status screen threading and
    keyboard handling are the responsibility of individual modes.








Initialization
  Sequence



  
1.

  
Keepawake Validation & Setup:

  
 •

  
Validates mutual exclusivity of --keepawake and --no-keepawake

  
 •

  
Enables keepawake if requested (default: off for server modes)

  
2.

  
Signal Handler Registration:

  
 •

  
Stores mode's interrupt_fn in static scope for signal context

  
 •

  
Registers SIGINT and SIGTERM to call server_like_signal_wrapper()

  
 •

  
Ignores SIGPIPE (TCP/WebSocket friendly)

  
 •

  
interrupt_fn must be async-signal-safe and close listening sockets

  
3.

  
Mode-Specific Initialization:

  
 •

  
Calls config->init_fn(config->init_user_data)

  
 •

  
If init_fn fails, returns error code and proceeds to cleanup








Main
  Run Loop


Calls config->run_fn(config->run_user_data), which blocks
    until server shuts down. This is typically an accept loop (tcp_server_run,
    acds_server_run, etc.).








Cleanup
  (always runs, even on error)



  
•

  
Disables keepawake if it was enabled during init








Notes


    
  
  • Status screen threading is not managed by this layer. Modes that
      need status display should implement their own status_fn callback and
      thread management within run_fn.
    • 
  
  • The config->status_fn field is available for mode-specific use but is
      not invoked by server_like itself.
    • 



Parameters


Config Module Mode configuration.
  Must not be NULL. init_fn, run_fn, and interrupt_fn must not be
  NULL.


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, or the first error code from
  initialization or from run_fn. Cleanup always runs regardless of return
  value.


Definition at line 38 of file server_like.c.


38                                                                                       {
39   if (!config) {
40     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config is NULL");
41   }
42 
43   if (!config->init_fn) {
44     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "init_fn is NULL");
45   }
46 
47   if (!config->run_fn) {
48     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "run_fn is NULL");
49   }
50 
51   if (!config->interrupt_fn) {
52     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "interrupt_fn is NULL");
53   }
54 
55   asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
56 
57   /* === INITIALIZATION === */
58 
59   /* 1. Validate keepawake mutual exclusivity */
60   bool keepawake_enabled = GET_OPTION(enable_keepawake);
61   bool keepawake_disabled = GET_OPTION(disable_keepawake);
62   if (keepawake_enabled && keepawake_disabled) {
63     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
64                      "Cannot specify both --keepawake and --no-keepawake");
65   }
66 
67   /* 2. Enable keepawake if requested (default is off for servers) */
68   if (keepawake_enabled) {
69     platform_enable_keepawake();
70   }
71 
72   /* 3. Store interrupt callback for signal handlers */
73   g_server_like_interrupt_fn = config->interrupt_fn;
74 
75   /* 4. Register signal handlers - note: signal handlers call interrupt_fn */
76   platform_signal(SIGINT, (signal_handler_t)server_like_signal_wrapper);
77   platform_signal(SIGTERM, (signal_handler_t)server_like_signal_wrapper);
78   platform_signal(SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN);
79 
80   /* 5. Call mode-specific initialization */
81   result = config->init_fn(config->init_user_data);
82   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
83     log_error("Mode initialization failed");
84     goto cleanup;
85   }
86 
87   /* === RUN === */
88 
89   /* 6. Call mode-specific blocking run loop */
90   result = config->run_fn(config->run_user_data);
91 
92 cleanup:
93   /* === CLEANUP === */
94 
95   /* 7. Disable keepawake */
96   if (keepawake_enabled) {
97     platform_disable_keepawake();
98   }
99 
100   return result;
101 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    GET_OPTION, session_server_like_config::init_fn,
    session_server_like_config::init_user_data,
    session_server_like_config::interrupt_fn, log_error,
    platform_disable_keepawake(), platform_enable_keepawake(),
    platform_signal(), session_server_like_config::run_fn,
    session_server_like_config::run_user_data, and SET_ERRNO.

asciichat_error_t session_settings_deserialize (const
  uint8_t * buffer, size_t len, session_settings_t *
  settings)


#include <src/common/session/settings.h>


Deserialize session settings from binary buffer.


Parameters


buffer Input buffer containing serialized settings


len Length of input buffer in bytes


settings Output settings structure (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Deserializes settings from binary format. Validates buffer length
    and extracts fields in network byte order.




Note


Returns ERROR_INVALID_PARAM if buffer is too small.


Definition at line 101 of file settings.c.


101                                                                                                                 {
102   if (!buffer || !settings) {
103     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_settings_deserialize: NULL parameter");
104   }
105 
106   // Check minimum buffer size
107   if (len < SESSION_SETTINGS_SERIALIZED_SIZE) {
108     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_settings_deserialize: buffer too small (%zu < %d)", len,
109                      SESSION_SETTINGS_SERIALIZED_SIZE);
110   }
111 
112   memset(settings, 0, sizeof(session_settings_t));
113   size_t offset = 0;
114 
115   // Version (4 bytes, network byte order)
116   uint32_t version_net;
117   memcpy(&version_net, buffer + offset, sizeof(version_net));
118   settings->version = ntohl(version_net);
119   offset += sizeof(version_net);
120 
121   // Width (2 bytes, network byte order)
122   uint16_t width_net;
123   memcpy(&width_net, buffer + offset, sizeof(width_net));
124   settings->width = (int16_t)ntohs(width_net);
125   offset += sizeof(width_net);
126 
127   // Height (2 bytes, network byte order)
128   uint16_t height_net;
129   memcpy(&height_net, buffer + offset, sizeof(height_net));
130   settings->height = (int16_t)ntohs(height_net);
131   offset += sizeof(height_net);
132 
133   // Color mode (1 byte)
134   settings->color_mode = buffer[offset++];
135 
136   // Render mode (1 byte)
137   settings->render_mode = buffer[offset++];
138 
139   // Palette type (1 byte)
140   settings->palette_type = buffer[offset++];
141 
142   // Custom palette (32 bytes)
143   memcpy(settings->palette_custom, buffer + offset, 32);
144   settings->palette_custom[31] = '\0'; // Ensure null termination
145   offset += 32;
146 
147   // Audio enabled (1 byte)
148   settings->audio_enabled = buffer[offset++];
149 
150   // Encryption required (1 byte)
151   settings->encryption_required = buffer[offset++];
152 
153   // Reserved (16 bytes)
154   memcpy(settings->reserved, buffer + offset, 16);
155   // offset += 16; // Unused, commented to avoid warning
156 
157   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
158 }

160                                                                               {

162     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_settings_from_options: NULL settings");
163   }
164 
165   // Initialize to defaults
166   session_settings_init(settings);
167 
168   // Get current options
169   const options_t *opts = options_get();
170   if (!opts) {
171     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Options not initialized");
172   }
173 
174   // Set version to current time for ordering
175   settings->version = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
176 
177   // Copy dimension settings
178   settings->width = (int16_t)opts->width;
179   settings->height = (int16_t)opts->height;
180 
181   // Copy display settings
182   settings->color_mode = (uint8_t)opts->color_mode;
183   settings->render_mode = (uint8_t)opts->render_mode;
184   settings->palette_type = (uint8_t)opts->palette_type;
185 
186   // Copy custom palette if set
187   if (opts->palette_custom_set && opts->palette_custom[0] != '\0') {
188     SAFE_STRNCPY(settings->palette_custom, opts->palette_custom, sizeof(settings->palette_custom));
189   }
190 
191   // Copy audio/encryption settings
192   settings->audio_enabled = (uint8_t)opts->audio_enabled;
193   settings->encryption_required = opts->no_encrypt ? 0 : 1;
194 
195   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
196 }

    • 
  
  • Color mode: TERM_COLOR_AUTO
    • 
  
  • Render mode: RENDER_MODE_FOREGROUND

asciichat_error_t session_settings_serialize (const
  session_settings_t * settings, uint8_t * buffer, size_t *
  len)


#include <src/common/session/settings.h>


Serialize session settings to binary buffer.


Parameters


settings Settings to serialize (must not be NULL)


buffer Output buffer (must be at least SESSION_SETTINGS_SERIALIZED_SIZE
  bytes)


len Output parameter for actual serialized length (must not be
  NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Serializes settings to a compact binary format suitable for
    network transmission. Uses network byte order for multi-byte integers.


Definition at line 51 of file settings.c.


51                                                                                                                {
52   if (!settings || !buffer || !len) {
53     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_settings_serialize: NULL parameter");
54   }
55 
56   // Fixed-size serialization format
57   size_t offset = 0;
58 
59   // Version (4 bytes, network byte order)
60   uint32_t version_net = htonl(settings->version);
61   memcpy(buffer + offset, &version_net, sizeof(version_net));
62   offset += sizeof(version_net);
63 
64   // Width (2 bytes, network byte order)
65   uint16_t width_net = htons((uint16_t)(settings->width & 0xFFFF));
66   memcpy(buffer + offset, &width_net, sizeof(width_net));
67   offset += sizeof(width_net);
68 
69   // Height (2 bytes, network byte order)
70   uint16_t height_net = htons((uint16_t)(settings->height & 0xFFFF));
71   memcpy(buffer + offset, &height_net, sizeof(height_net));
72   offset += sizeof(height_net);
73 
74   // Color mode (1 byte)
75   buffer[offset++] = settings->color_mode;
76 
77   // Render mode (1 byte)
78   buffer[offset++] = settings->render_mode;
79 
80   // Palette type (1 byte)
81   buffer[offset++] = settings->palette_type;
82 
83   // Custom palette (32 bytes, null-padded)
84   memcpy(buffer + offset, settings->palette_custom, 32);
85   offset += 32;
86 
87   // Audio enabled (1 byte)
88   buffer[offset++] = settings->audio_enabled;
89 
90   // Encryption required (1 byte)
91   buffer[offset++] = settings->encryption_required;
92 
93   // Reserved (16 bytes)
94   memcpy(buffer + offset, settings->reserved, 16);
95   offset += 16;
96 
97   *len = offset;
98   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
99 }

void
  splash_clear_display_context (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/ui/splash.h>


Clear the cached display context to prevent use-after-free. Must
    be called when the display context is being destroyed to prevent worker
    threads from accessing freed memory. This clears the reference stored in
    g_splash_state.




Note


Thread-safe (uses atomic operations)



Safe to call multiple times

int
  splash_intro_done (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/ui/splash.h>


Signal that intro splash should end (first frame ready to render).
    Tells the splash screen animation to stop displaying. On the next animation
    frame attempt, it will clear the screen and exit cleanly.




Important: Call splash_wait_for_animation() after
    this to ensure the animation thread has fully exited before rendering ASCII
    art.




Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Note


Safe to call multiple times



After calling this, next frame render should show real ASCII
  art




The animation thread checks this flag and exits gracefully




MUST call splash_wait_for_animation() before displaying
    ASCII frames




Definition at line 692 of file splash.c.


692                             {
693   // Record when intro_done was called - animation thread will use this to decide when to stop
694   // Animation thread needs time to check the flag and perform cleanup, so this must happen
695   // BEFORE we try to access the terminal for frame rendering
696   atomic_store_u64(&g_splash_state.intro_done_time_ns, time_get_ns());
697 
698   // NOTE: Do NOT modify thread_created flag here!
699   // splash_wait_for_animation() needs to see thread_created==true to know whether to join the thread.
700   // If we set thread_created=false here, splash_wait_for_animation() won't join, and the render loop
701   // will start while the animation thread is still running, causing frame output to conflict with splash.
702   //
703   // Instead, just signal the animation thread to stop via intro_done_time_ns, and let
704   // splash_wait_for_animation() handle the actual thread join.
705 
706   // NOTE: DO NOT set is_running = false here! The animation thread sets it to false
707   // when it actually exits (line 633). If we set it to false here, the render loop
708   // guard will think splash is done while the animation thread is still running for
709   // up to 2 more seconds, allowing ASCII frames to render simultaneously with splash.
710   // The is_running flag MUST only be cleared by the animation thread when it finishes.
711 
712   return 0;
713 }






References atomic_store_u64(), and
  time_get_ns().


Referenced by display_disable_logging_for_first_frame(),
    session_client_like_run(), and
  session_display_write_ascii().






int
  splash_intro_start (session_display_ctx_t * ctx)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ui/splash.h>


Start animated intro screen with rainbow ASCII art (non-blocking).
    Starts the splash screen animation that runs while initialization happens in
    the background. The animation continues until splash_intro_done() is
    called.




Display Requirements:


    
  
  • stdin AND stdout must be TTY (platform_isatty())
    • 
  
  • --no-intro-screen option must not be set
    • 
  
  • Not in snapshot mode (--snapshot)
    • 
  
  • Terminal must be large enough (min 50 cols x 20 rows)
    • 



Behavior:


    
  
  • Starts animated rainbow border with "ascii-chat" logo
    • 
  
  • Runs continuously until splash_intro_done() is called
    • 
  
  • Animation loops with rainbow color cycling
    • 
  
  • When splash_intro_done() called, animation stops on next frame
    • 
  
  • Next real frame render clears screen and shows ASCII art
    • 



Parameters


ctx Display context (can be NULL for minimal
  stdout-only mode)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Note


Non-blocking - returns immediately after starting
  animation



Call splash_intro_done() when first frame is ready to
    render




Uses lock-free option access via GET_OPTION() macro




Definition at line 638 of file splash.c.


638                                                    {
639   (void)ctx; // Not needed - display context is not actively used
640 
641   // Check if splash should display
642   if (!splash_should_display(true)) {
643     return 0;
644   }
645 
646   // Check terminal size
647   int width = (int)terminal_get_effective_width();
648   int height = (int)terminal_get_effective_height();
649   if (width < 50 || height < 20) {
650     return 0;
651   }
652 
653   // Redirect splash screen to stderr when stdout is piped (not a TTY).
654   // This keeps stdout clean for frame data while splash goes to stderr.
655   // Uses platform_isatty() so non-desktop platforms (iOS, WASM) can override.
656   if (!platform_isatty(STDOUT_FILENO)) {
657     terminal_screen_set_output_fd(STDERR_FILENO);
658   }
659 
660   // Initialize log buffer (same pattern as server_status)
661   splash_log_init();
662 
663   // Clear screen and show cursor
664   terminal_clear_screen();
665   (void)terminal_cursor_show();
666 
667   // Clear log buffer for clean slate
668   splash_log_clear();
669 
670   // Set running flag
671   atomic_store_bool(&g_splash_state.is_running, true);
672   atomic_store_bool(&g_splash_state.should_stop, false);
673   g_splash_state.frame = 0;
674   g_splash_state.start_time_ns = time_get_ns();
675 
676   // Start animation thread (only create once)
677   bool expected_false = false;
678   if (!atomic_cas_bool(&g_splash_state.thread_created, &expected_false, true)) {
679     return 0;
680   }
681 
682   int err = asciichat_thread_create(&g_splash_state.anim_thread, "splash_anim", splash_animation_thread, NULL);
683   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
684     log_warn("Failed to create splash animation thread: error=%d", err);
685     atomic_store_bool(&g_splash_state.thread_created, false);
686     return 0;
687   }
688 
689   return 0; // ASCIICHAT_OK
690 }

#include <include/ascii-chat/ui/splash.h>


Check if splash screen animation is currently running. Returns
    true if the splash animation thread is actively rendering. Used to prevent
    ASCII art frame rendering from overlapping with the splash.

Note


Thread-safe (uses atomic operations)

void
  splash_notify_first_frame (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/ui/splash.h>


Notify splash that the first frame has been rendered. Called from
    display_render_frame() when the first ASCII frame is rendered. This
    updates the splash state's atomic flag so the splash animation knows when to
    stop.




Important: This function does NOT access the display
    context pointer, making it safe to call from any thread even if the context
    is being freed.




Note


Thread-safe (uses atomic operations)



Safe to call multiple times




Must NOT dereference display_ctx (unlike splash_intro_done)








void
  splash_restore_stderr (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/ui/splash.h>


Restore stderr after splash animation completes. Restores the
    original stderr file descriptor that was saved when
    splash_intro_start() was called. This must be called after splash
    animation completes to allow debug logging to appear on screen again.




Note


Safe to call multiple times (idempotent)



Must only be called after splash_intro_start() was
  called

776   if (lifecycle_init_once(&g_update_notification_lifecycle)) {
777     mutex_init(&g_update_notification_mutex, "update_notification");
778     lifecycle_init_commit(&g_update_notification_lifecycle);
779   }
780   mutex_lock(&g_update_notification_mutex);
781

783     g_update_notification[0] = '\0';
784     log_debug("Cleared update notification for splash/status screens");
785   } else {
786     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_update_notification, notification, sizeof(g_update_notification));
787     log_debug("Set update notification for splash/status screens: %s", notification);
788   }
789 
790   mutex_unlock(&g_update_notification_mutex);
791 }

bool splash_should_display (bool is_intro)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ui/splash.h>


Check if splash screen should display. Determines if TTY checks
    pass and options allow splash display. Respects both TTY detection and
    option flags.




Parameters


is_intro true for intro screen checks, false for
  status screen


Returns


true if splash should display, false if should skip


Definition at line 398 of file splash.c.


398                                           {
399   // Check option flags (display splash if enabled, regardless of TTY for testing)
400   if (is_intro) {
401     // Allow splash in snapshot mode if loading from URL/file (has loading time)
402     // Skip splash only for quick webcam snapshots (unless explicitly enabled)
403     bool splash_screen_opt = GET_OPTION(splash_screen);
404     bool splash_explicitly_set = GET_OPTION(splash_screen_explicitly_set);
405     bool is_snapshot = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
406     bool has_media = (GET_OPTION(media_url) && strlen(GET_OPTION(media_url)) > 0) ||
407                      (GET_OPTION(media_file) && strlen(GET_OPTION(media_file)) > 0);
408 
409     // Show splash if enabled, but in snapshot mode only if:
410     // 1. Has media file/URL, OR
411     // 2. Explicitly enabled with --splash-screen=true
412     bool should_display = splash_screen_opt && (!is_snapshot || has_media || splash_explicitly_set);
413     // Show splash if:
414     // 1. Not in snapshot mode, OR
415     // 2. In snapshot mode but loading from URL/file (needs splash during load)
416     return should_display;
417   } else {
418     return GET_OPTION(status_screen);
419   }
420 }

void
  splash_wait_for_animation (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/ui/splash.h>


Wait for splash animation thread to fully exit. Blocks until the
    splash animation thread has completed and exited. Must be called after
    splash_intro_done() and before rendering ASCII frames to prevent the
    splash and ASCII art from appearing simultaneously.




Note


Safe to call multiple times



Blocks on the animation thread join




MUST be called before first display_render_frame() call




Definition at line 730 of file splash.c.


730                                      {
731   // Wait for animation thread to fully exit before rendering ASCII art
732   // This prevents the splash and ASCII art from appearing simultaneously
733   //
734   // The animation thread will exit gracefully when:
735   // - Intro done signal received AND 2 seconds have elapsed, OR
736   // - 30 seconds have elapsed (safety timeout)
737   // - OR a shutdown signal is received
738   //
739   // We block here indefinitely to ensure the splash animation completes before ASCII art starts
740 
741   // Only join if we successfully created the thread
742   if (atomic_load_bool(&g_splash_state.thread_created)) {
743     log_dev("[SPLASH_WAIT] Waiting for animation thread to exit...");
744 
745     // Check if shutdown was requested - handle it specially
746     bool is_shutting_down = shutdown_is_requested();
747     if (is_shutting_down) {
748       log_dev("[SPLASH_WAIT] Shutdown requested, signaling animation thread to stop");
749       atomic_store_bool(&g_splash_state.should_stop, true);
750       // During shutdown, use a short timeout (100ms)
751       uint64_t timeout_ns = 100LL * NS_PER_MS_INT;
752       (void)asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_splash_state.anim_thread, NULL, timeout_ns);
753     } else {
754       // Normal operation: wait indefinitely for the animation thread to finish
755       // This ensures splash animation is 100% done before ASCII art rendering starts
756       asciichat_error_t err = asciichat_thread_join(&g_splash_state.anim_thread, NULL);
757       if (err == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
758         log_dev("[SPLASH_WAIT] Animation thread exited cleanly");
759       } else {
760         log_warn("[SPLASH_WAIT] Animation thread join failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
761         // Force stop the animation thread if join failed
762         atomic_store_bool(&g_splash_state.should_stop, true);
763         // Clear any partial splash output left on screen
764         terminal_clear_screen();
765         terminal_cursor_home(STDOUT_FILENO);
766         terminal_flush(STDOUT_FILENO);
767       }
768     }
769 
770     // Mark that we've joined (safe to call multiple times - only joins once)
771     atomic_store_bool(&g_splash_state.thread_created, false);
772   }
773 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, asciichat_thread_join(),
    asciichat_thread_join_timeout(), atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_store_bool(), log_dev, log_warn,
    NS_PER_MS_INT, shutdown_is_requested(),
    terminal_clear_screen(), terminal_cursor_home(), and
    terminal_flush().
